revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 田乃家神社・田乃家御前神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Tanoe-jinja-Tanoemimae-jinja.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]矢野字宮前387 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 36.4 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 36 | 経度秒 = 16.2 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 田乃家神社：大神御滄川神
田乃家御前神社：御前神 |社格= 田乃家神社：式内社（小）、{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}}摂社
田乃家御前神社：皇大神宮摂社 |創建= |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 当藪 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Tanoe Shrine''' (田乃家神社) is a {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrine)|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} (Naikū) within {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. This article also covers the '''Tanoe-Mimae Shrine''' (田乃家御前神社), which shares the same precinct as Tanoe Shrine. Both shrines enshrine deities that serve as {{ill|guardian deities|ja|守護神}} of {{ill|farmhouses|ja|農家}}Gakken Publishing (2013): page 69. [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[ja:田乃家神社]] == 概要 == ここでは2社共通事項について記す。 [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]矢野字宮前387に鎮座する式内社研究会 編（1990）：211ページ。鎮座地の矢野地区は、[[斎宮|斎王]]・稚足姫皇女（わかたらしひめのひめみこ）の最期の地であるという説がある{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.meiwa.mie.jp/romantic/saio/saio4.html |title=斎王ゆかりの地（町外編） - 明和町役場 | publisher=三重県明和町 |language=日本語 |accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2045-21/www.town.meiwa.mie.jp/romantic/saio/saio4.html |archivedate=2013-11-03}}。[[境内]]には[[古墳]]が4基ある金子（1983）：61ページと『中南勢開発地域遺跡地図』にあるが、木本雅文による実地調査によれば、5基確認されたという。鎮座地の矢野付近からは[[弥生時代]]・[[古墳時代]]の[[遺跡]]が多数発見されている。また[[享保]]9年（1724年）に[[紀州藩]]が建立した「禁殺生石」、[[天保]]13年（1842年）の銘のある水盤が境内にある{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area04/03.html?iframe=true&width=1000&height=700 |title=田乃家御前神社 - 125社｜式年遷宮記念 せんぐう館 | publisher=せんぐう館 |language=日本語 |accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2159-39/www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area04/03.html?iframe=true&width=1000&height=700 |archivedate=2013-11-03}}。 2社は同座しており、[[社殿]]は1つである。[[神明造]][[板葺]]の社殿は[[南]]の方角を向いており、一重の[[玉垣]]に囲まれている。1基の[[神明鳥居]]がある。[[賽銭]]箱は置かれていない。社地の[[面積]]は13,114[[平方メートル|m2]]。 == 田乃家神社 == 田乃家神社は、内宮の摂社27社のうち第5位である宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：9 - 11ページ。内宮摂社の中でも格式の高い神社である伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：65ページ。[[神体]]は[[鏡]]である金子（1983）：59ページ。 [[祭神]]は大神御滄川神（おおかみのみさむかわのかみ）。[[農耕]]者と[[住宅|家]]の守護神である。「大神」と名が付いているのは、皇大神宮の祭神・[[アマテラス|天照大御神]]の神徳がこの地域に及び定められた神社であることに由来する。度会延経は、「大神御滄川神」の滄を「うみ」と読み、天照大御神の[[荒魂・和魂|和魂]]であり、[[水神|水霊]]であると解釈した式内社研究会 編（1990）：210ページ。 社名の「田乃家」（たのえ）は、「田の上」という[[地形]]説、「田邊郷」という[[地名]]説、「田戸」（[[公田]]のそばに住む人々の代々の田や戸）説がある式内社研究会 編（1990）：208ページ。木本雅文は『式内社調査報告』において地名説には疑義があるとし、「田の上」説と「田戸」説はどちらが正しいか検証できる[[史料]]がないと述べている式内社研究会 編（1990）：209ページ。また、『皇大神宮儀式帳』に記載のある社名「田邊神社」から文字通り田のほとり・近く（辺）にある神社とも解釈できるとした。せんぐう館のウェブサイトや『伊勢神宮に行こう』では、「田戸」説のみ紹介している。 [[御巫清直]]は「田邊神社」と「田邊氏神社」（氏神社、内宮[[禰宜]]を務めた[[荒木田氏]]の祖先を祀る）を同一視しているが、木本雅文は両神社の所在地や祭神が異なることから明らかに別の神社であると考えられると述べている式内社研究会 編（1990）：208 - 209ページ。一方、和田年弥は『日本の神々』の田乃家神社の項で以下のように記し、「田邊神社」と「氏神社」を同一視している谷川 編（1986）：66 - 67ページ。（以下の「当社」は「田乃家神社」を指す。） {{Cquote|田辺は皇大神宮を奉斎する荒木田氏の本貫地と推定されており、「氏社」あるいは「氏神社」とも称されるように荒木田氏の祖霊を祀る当社は、数ある神宮の摂末社のなかでも特異な存在である。}} == 田乃家御前神社 == 田乃家御前神社は、内宮の摂社27社のうち第6位である。正殿は中絶し、田乃家神社に同座する宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：10ページ。 祭神は御前神（みまえのかみ）。大神御滄川神と同じく農家の守護神である。祭神の名は、「大神御滄川御前神」（おおかみのみさむかわのみまえのかみ）、「田乃家御前神」とも表記する。 == 歴史 == 『皇大神宮儀式帳』によれば、[[雄略天皇]]の御代に創建されたという金子（1983）：68ページ。確実なところでは、伊勢神宮の摂社の定義より『[[延喜式神名帳]]』成立、すなわち[[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）以前に創建された伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：22ページ。『皇大神宮儀式帳』にも記載があることから[[延暦]]23年（[[804年]]）以前から存在したことになる。なお、『皇大神宮儀式帳』には「田邊神社」または「田邊社」と記されている。 [[古代]]の田乃家神社は皇大神宮に準じて20年に一度の[[Jingū Shikinen Sengū|式年遷宮]]の対象となっていた。しかし[[暦応]]3年[[12月8日 (旧暦)|12月8日]]（ユリウス暦：[[1340年]][[12月27日]]）に[[若狭国]]・[[丹波国]]の[[役夫工米]]をもって[[社殿]]を造り替えるよう命じた記録以後、遷宮の記録は途絶え、祭祀も断絶、社地すら不明となってしまった。[[寛文]]3年[[9月8日 (旧暦)|9月8日]]（[[グレゴリオ暦]]：[[1663年]][[10月8日]]）、大宮司の[[河辺精長|河邊精長]]（大中臣精長）により再興された。この時、田乃家御前神社は再興されず、以後田乃家神社と同座することとなった。精長が比定した場所からは鳥居跡と考えられる丸石や社地に敷かれていたと思われる白石が多数出土したことから、旧社地として正しいと考えられるが、「田邊神社」と「氏神社」を同一視する御巫清直は田宮寺境内の天神が旧地であると主張した式内社研究会 編（1990）：210 - 211ページ。 社殿は[[1920年]]（大正9年）4月に造り替えられた。 == 祭祀 == 巡回祭典の形で[[祈年祭]]（2月）、[[月次祭]]（6月・12月）、[[神嘗祭]]（10月）、[[新嘗祭]]（11月）の際は社殿の前で祭祀が行われ、[[歳旦祭]]（1月）、[[元始祭]]（1月）、建国記念祭（[[2月11日]]）、風日祈祭（5月・8月）、[[天長祭]]（[[12月23日]]）は内宮五丈殿で遥祀にて催行する。 地域の習俗として、[[3月16日]]の当藪（とうやぶ）[[庚申待|日待]]、[[11月11日]]の鳥名子組の日待が行われる櫻井（1991）：293ページ。当藪は、田乃家神社の俗称である。 == 交通 == 神社前には入り口となる道路が2本ある{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html |title=コース19 玉城ルート2 〜古薫る田園の神々を巡る〜 | publisher=伊勢市観光協会 |language=日本語 |accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2025-45/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html |archivedate=2013-11-03}}。伊勢市観光協会によれば、入り口は少し分かりづらい。[[1980年代]]には[[伊勢市駅]]から玉城町の野中行きの[[路線バス]]が運行され、「矢野」[[バス停留所|バス停]]が最寄りの停留所であった。 * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[参宮線]] ** [[外城田駅]]より徒歩約40分（約3.2[[キロメートル|km]]）。 ** [[田丸駅]]より徒歩約42分（約3.4[[キロメートル|km]]）。 * [[伊勢自動車道]][[玉城インターチェンジ|玉城IC]]より約10分三重県観光連盟"[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_8283.html 田乃家神社の観光施設・周辺情報 - 観光三重]"（2013年11月3日閲覧。）。付近に[[駐車場]]はない。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3年11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[谷川健一]] 編『日本の神々―神社と聖地 第六巻 伊勢・志摩・伊賀・紀伊』[[白水社]]、1986年3月31日、492p. ISBN 4-560-02216-X == 関連項目 == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_02.jpg 田乃家神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0705.html 田乃家神社（たのえじんじゃ） 御同座 田乃家御前神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|たのえしんしや}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:田乃家神社]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:田乃家神社]][[ja:田乃家神社]][[Category:untranslated pages]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:田乃家神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:田乃家神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:new pages]] [[en:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]]

{{神社 |名称= 田乃家神社・田乃家御前神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Tanoe-jinja-Tanoemimae-jinja.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]矢野字宮前387 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 36.4 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 36 | 経度秒 = 16.2 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 田乃家神社：大神御滄川神
田乃家御前神社：御前神 |社格= 田乃家神社：式内社（小）、{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}}摂社
田乃家御前神社：皇大神宮摂社 |創建= |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 当藪 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Tanoe Shrine''' (田乃家神社) is a {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrine)|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} (Naikū) within {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. This article also covers the '''Tanoe-Mimae Shrine''' (田乃家御前神社), which shares the same precinct as Tanoe Shrine. Both shrines enshrine deities that serve as {{ill|guardian deities|ja|守護神}} of {{ill|farmhouses|ja|農家}}Gakken Publishing (2013): page 69. [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[ja:田乃家神社]] == Overview == This section describes common information about both shrines. Located at {{ill|Tamaki|ja|玉城町}}, {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Yano Aza Miyamae 387Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 211. There is a theory that the Yano area, where the shrine is situated, is the final resting place of {{ill|Princess Wakatara|ja|稚足姫皇女}}, who served as a {{ill|Saio|ja|斎王}}{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.town.meiwa.mie.jp/romantic/saio/saio4.html|title=Sites Associated with the Saio (Outside of Town) - Meiwa Town Office|publisher=Meiwa Town, Mie Prefecture|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2045-21/www.town.meiwa.mie.jp/romantic/saio/saio4.html|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. According to the "Chūnansei Development Area Archaeological Map," there are four {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mounds) within the precinctsKaneko (1983): p. 61, but field research by Masafumi Kimoto confirmed five. Many {{ill|archaeological sites|ja|遺跡}} from the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} and {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} have been discovered in the Yano area. Additionally, within the precincts are the "Prohibited Killing Stone" established by the {{ill|Kishu Domain|ja|紀州藩}} in {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}} 9 (1724) and a water basin dated {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 13 (1842){{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area04/03.html?iframe=true&width=1000&height=700|title=Tanoya Gozen Shrine - 125 Shrines｜Sengukan|publisher=Sengukan|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2159-39/www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area04/03.html?iframe=true&width=1000&height=700|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}} constructed in the {{ill|shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} architectural style with a wooden shingle roof, facing south and enclosed by a single layer of {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} (sacred fence). There is one {{ill|shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居}}. No {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭箱}} is present. The shrine grounds cover an area of 13,114 m². == Tanoya Shrine == Tanoya Shrine ranks 5th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Inner Shrine (Kōtai Jingū)|ja|皇大神宮}}Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11. It holds a high status among the auxiliary shrines of the Inner ShrineIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 65. Its {{ill|sacred object|ja|神体}} is a {{ill|mirror|ja|鏡}}Kaneko (1983): p. 59. The {{ill|enshrined deity|ja|祭神}} is Ōkami Misamukawa-no-Kami (大神御滄川神). The deity is revered as the guardian of {{ill|farmers|ja|農耕}} and {{ill|households|ja|住宅}}. The prefix "Ōkami" signifies the extension of the divine virtues of {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神}}, the main deity of the Inner Shrine, to this region. The shrine name "Tanoya" has several theories regarding its origin: one suggests it means "on the rice field" (田の上), another links it to the place name "Tanabe" (田邊郷), and a third relates it to "Tato" (田戸), referring to rice fields and residences near government-owned paddiesShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 208. == Tanoya Gozen Shrine == Tanoya Gozen Shrine ranks 6th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine. Its main hall no longer exists, and the deity is now enshrined together with Tanoya ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10. The enshrined deity is Gozen-no-Kami (御前神). Like Ōkami Misamukawa-no-Kami, this deity is also a guardian of farmers. == History == According to the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū Ritual Records|ja|皇大神宮儀式帳}}, the shrine was founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}Kaneko (1983): p. 68. Based on the definition of Ise Jingū's auxiliary shrines, it was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, i.e., before {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. In ancient times, Tanoya Shrine was subject to the {{ill|Shikinen Sengū|ja|式年遷宮}}, rebuilt every 20 years, just like the Inner Shrine. However, after an order to rebuild the shrine in {{ill|Ryakuō|ja|暦応}} 3 (1340), no further records of reconstruction exist, and both the rituals and the shrine site fell into obscurity. The shrine was restored on {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 3, 8th day of the 9th month (October 8, 1663, Gregorian calendar) by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga). == Rituals == Shinto rituals are held in front of the shrine during major festivals such as the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November). == Access == There are two entrance roads to the shrine{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=Course 19 Tamaki Route 2 - Visiting the Gods of the Ancient Countryside|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2025-45/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}} * Approximately 40 minutes on foot from {{ill|Sotoida Station|ja|外城田駅}} (~3.2 km). * Approximately 42 minutes on foot from {{ill|Tamura Station|ja|田丸駅}} (~3.4 km). * About 10 minutes from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "Tanoya Shrine Tourist Information - Mie Sightseeing" (Accessed November 3, 2013). [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[ja:田乃家神社]] == 関連項目 == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_02.jpg 田乃家神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0705.html 田乃家神社（たのえじんじゃ） 御同座 田乃家御前神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|たのえしんしや}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:田乃家神社]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:田乃家神社]][[ja:田乃家神社]][[Category:untranslated pages]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:田乃家神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:田乃家神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:new pages]] [[en:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 田乃家神社・田乃家御前神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Tanoe-jinja-Tanoemimae-jinja.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]矢野字宮前387 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 36.4 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 36 | 経度秒 = 16.2 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 田乃家神社：大神御滄川神
田乃家御前神社：御前神 |社格= 田乃家神社：式内社（小）、{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}}摂社
田乃家御前神社：皇大神宮摂社 |創建= |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 当藪 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Tanoe Shrine''' (田乃家神社) is a {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrine)|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} (Naikū) within {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. This article also covers the '''Tanoe-Mimae Shrine''' (田乃家御前神社), which shares the same precinct as Tanoe Shrine. Both shrines enshrine deities that serve as {{ill|guardian deities|ja|守護神}} of {{ill|farmhouses|ja|農家}}Gakken Publishing (2013): page 69. [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[ja:田乃家神社]] == Overview == This section describes common information about both shrines. Located at {{ill|Tamaki|ja|玉城町}}, {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Yano Aza Miyamae 387Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 211. There is a theory that the Yano area, where the shrine is situated, is the final resting place of {{ill|Princess Wakatara|ja|稚足姫皇女}}, who served as a {{ill|Saio|ja|斎王}}{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.town.meiwa.mie.jp/romantic/saio/saio4.html|title=Sites Associated with the Saio (Outside of Town) - Meiwa Town Office|publisher=Meiwa Town, Mie Prefecture|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2045-21/www.town.meiwa.mie.jp/romantic/saio/saio4.html|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. According to the "Chūnansei Development Area Archaeological Map," there are four {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mounds) within the precinctsKaneko (1983): p. 61, but field research by Masafumi Kimoto confirmed five. Many {{ill|archaeological sites|ja|遺跡}} from the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} and {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} have been discovered in the Yano area. Additionally, within the precincts are the "Prohibited Killing Stone" established by the {{ill|Kishu Domain|ja|紀州藩}} in {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}} 9 (1724) and a water basin dated {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 13 (1842){{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area04/03.html?iframe=true&width=1000&height=700|title=Tanoya Gozen Shrine - 125 Shrines｜Sengukan|publisher=Sengukan|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2159-39/www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area04/03.html?iframe=true&width=1000&height=700|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}} constructed in the {{ill|shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} architectural style with a wooden shingle roof, facing south and enclosed by a single layer of {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} (sacred fence). There is one {{ill|shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居}}. No {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭箱}} is present. The shrine grounds cover an area of 13,114 m². == Tanoya Shrine == Tanoya Shrine ranks 5th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Inner Shrine (Kōtai Jingū)|ja|皇大神宮}}Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11. It holds a high status among the auxiliary shrines of the Inner ShrineIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 65. Its {{ill|sacred object|ja|神体}} is a {{ill|mirror|ja|鏡}}Kaneko (1983): p. 59. The {{ill|enshrined deity|ja|祭神}} is Ōkami Misamukawa-no-Kami (大神御滄川神). The deity is revered as the guardian of {{ill|farmers|ja|農耕}} and {{ill|households|ja|住宅}}. The prefix "Ōkami" signifies the extension of the divine virtues of {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神}}, the main deity of the Inner Shrine, to this region. The shrine name "Tanoya" has several theories regarding its origin: one suggests it means "on the rice field" (田の上), another links it to the place name "Tanabe" (田邊郷), and a third relates it to "Tato" (田戸), referring to rice fields and residences near government-owned paddiesShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 208. == Tanoya Gozen Shrine == Tanoya Gozen Shrine ranks 6th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine. Its main hall no longer exists, and the deity is now enshrined together with Tanoya ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10. The enshrined deity is Gozen-no-Kami (御前神). Like Ōkami Misamukawa-no-Kami, this deity is also a guardian of farmers. == History == According to the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū Ritual Records|ja|皇大神宮儀式帳}}, the shrine was founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}Kaneko (1983): p. 68. Based on the definition of Ise Jingū's auxiliary shrines, it was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, i.e., before {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. In ancient times, Tanoya Shrine was subject to the {{ill|Shikinen Sengū|ja|式年遷宮}}, rebuilt every 20 years, just like the Inner Shrine. However, after an order to rebuild the shrine in {{ill|Ryakuō|ja|暦応}} 3 (1340), no further records of reconstruction exist, and both the rituals and the shrine site fell into obscurity. The shrine was restored on {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 3, 8th day of the 9th month (October 8, 1663, Gregorian calendar) by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga). == Rituals == Shinto rituals are held in front of the shrine during major festivals such as the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November). == Access == There are two entrance roads to the shrine{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=Course 19 Tamaki Route 2 - Visiting the Gods of the Ancient Countryside|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2025-45/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}} * Approximately 40 minutes on foot from {{ill|Sotoida Station|ja|外城田駅}} (~3.2 km). * Approximately 42 minutes on foot from {{ill|Tamura Station|ja|田丸駅}} (~3.4 km). * About 10 minutes from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "Tanoya Shrine Tourist Information - Mie Sightseeing" (Accessed November 3, 2013). [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[ja:田乃家神社]] == 関連項目 == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_02.jpg 田乃家神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0705.html 田乃家神社（たのえじんじゃ） 御同座 田乃家御前神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:田乃家神社]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:田乃家神社]][[ja:田乃家神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:田乃家神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:田乃家神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:new pages]] [[en:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]]

Its main hall no longer exists, and the deity is now enshrined together with Tanoya ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10. The enshrined deity is Gozen-no-Kami (御前神). Like Ōkami Misamukawa-no-Kami, this deity is also a guardian of farmers. == History == According to the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū Ritual Records|ja|皇大神宮儀式帳}}, the shrine was founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}Kaneko (1983): p. 68. Based on the definition of Ise Jingū's auxiliary shrines, it was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, i.e., before {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. In ancient times, Tanoya Shrine was subject to the {{ill|Shikinen Sengū|ja|式年遷宮}}, rebuilt every 20 years, just like the Inner Shrine. However, after an order to rebuild the shrine in {{ill|Ryakuō|ja|暦応}} 3 (1340), no further records of reconstruction exist, and both the rituals and the shrine site fell into obscurity. The shrine was restored on {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 3, 8th day of the 9th month (October 8, 1663, Gregorian calendar) by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga). == Rituals == Shinto rituals are held in front of the shrine during major festivals such as the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November). == Access == There are two entrance roads to the shrine{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=Course 19 Tamaki Route 2 - Visiting the Gods of the Ancient Countryside|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2025-45/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}} * Approximately 40 minutes on foot from {{ill|Sotoida Station|ja|外城田駅}} (~3.2 km). * Approximately 42 minutes on foot from {{ill|Tamura Station|ja|田丸駅}} (~3.4 km). * About 10 minutes from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "Tanoya Shrine Tourist Information - Mie Sightseeing" (Accessed November 3, 2013). [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[ja:田乃家神社]] == 関連項目 == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_02.jpg 田乃家神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0705.html 田乃家神社（たのえじんじゃ） 御同座 田乃家御前神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:田乃家神社]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:田乃家神社]][[ja:田乃家神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:田乃家神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:田乃家神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]]

{{神社 |名称= 田乃家神社・田乃家御前神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Tanoe-jinja-Tanoemimae-jinja.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]矢野字宮前387 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 36.4 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 36 | 経度秒 = 16.2 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 田乃家神社：大神御滄川神
田乃家御前神社：御前神 |社格= 田乃家神社：式内社（小）、{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}}摂社
田乃家御前神社：皇大神宮摂社 |創建= |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 当藪 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Tanoe Shrine''' (田乃家神社) is a {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrine)|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} (Naikū) within {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. This article also covers the '''Tanoe-Mimae Shrine''' (田乃家御前神社), which shares the same precinct as Tanoe Shrine. Both shrines enshrine deities that serve as {{ill|guardian deities|ja|守護神}} of {{ill|farmhouses|ja|農家}}Gakken Publishing (2013): page 69. [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[ja:田乃家神社]] == Overview == This section describes common information about both shrines. Located at {{ill|Tamaki|ja|玉城町}}, {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Yano Aza Miyamae 387Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 211. There is a theory that the Yano area, where the shrine is situated, is the final resting place of {{ill|Princess Wakatara|ja|稚足姫皇女}}, who served as a {{ill|Saio|ja|斎王}}{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.town.meiwa.mie.jp/romantic/saio/saio4.html|title=Sites Associated with the Saio (Outside of Town) - Meiwa Town Office|publisher=Meiwa Town, Mie Prefecture|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2045-21/www.town.meiwa.mie.jp/romantic/saio/saio4.html|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. According to the "Chūnansei Development Area Archaeological Map," there are four {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mounds) within the precinctsKaneko (1983): p. 61, but field research by Masafumi Kimoto confirmed five. Many {{ill|archaeological sites|ja|遺跡}} from the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} and {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} have been discovered in the Yano area. Additionally, within the precincts are the "Prohibited Killing Stone" established by the {{ill|Kishu Domain|ja|紀州藩}} in {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}} 9 (1724) and a water basin dated {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 13 (1842){{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area04/03.html?iframe=true&width=1000&height=700|title=Tanoya Gozen Shrine - 125 Shrines｜Sengukan|publisher=Sengukan|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2159-39/www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area04/03.html?iframe=true&width=1000&height=700|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}} constructed in the {{ill|shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} architectural style with a wooden shingle roof, facing south and enclosed by a single layer of {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} (sacred fence). There is one {{ill|shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居}}. No {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭箱}} is present. The shrine grounds cover an area of 13,114 m². == Tanoya Shrine == Tanoya Shrine ranks 5th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Inner Shrine (Kōtai Jingū)|ja|皇大神宮}}Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11. It holds a high status among the auxiliary shrines of the Inner ShrineIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 65. Its {{ill|sacred object|ja|神体}} is a {{ill|mirror|ja|鏡}}Kaneko (1983): p. 59. The {{ill|enshrined deity|ja|祭神}} is Ōkami Misamukawa-no-Kami (大神御滄川神). The deity is revered as the guardian of {{ill|farmers|ja|農耕}} and {{ill|households|ja|住宅}}. The prefix "Ōkami" signifies the extension of the divine virtues of {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神}}, the main deity of the Inner Shrine, to this region. The shrine name "Tanoya" has several theories regarding its origin: one suggests it means "on the rice field" (田の上), another links it to the place name "Tanabe" (田邊郷), and a third relates it to "Tato" (田戸), referring to rice fields and residences near government-owned paddiesShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 208. == Tanoya Gozen Shrine == Tanoya Gozen Shrine ranks 6th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine. Its main hall no longer exists, and the deity is now enshrined together with Tanoya ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10. The enshrined deity is Gozen-no-Kami (御前神). Like Ōkami Misamukawa-no-Kami, this deity is also a guardian of farmers. == History == According to the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū Ritual Records|ja|皇大神宮儀式帳}}, the shrine was founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}Kaneko (1983): p. 68. Based on the definition of Ise Jingū's auxiliary shrines, it was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, i.e., before {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. In ancient times, Tanoya Shrine was subject to the {{ill|Shikinen Sengū|ja|式年遷宮}}, rebuilt every 20 years, just like the Inner Shrine. However, after an order to rebuild the shrine in {{ill|Ryakuō|ja|暦応}} 3 (1340), no further records of reconstruction exist, and both the rituals and the shrine site fell into obscurity. The shrine was restored on {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 3, 8th day of the 9th month (October 8, 1663, Gregorian calendar) by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga). == Rituals == Shinto rituals are held in front of the shrine during major festivals such as the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November). == Access == There are two entrance roads to the shrine{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=Course 19 Tamaki Route 2 - Visiting the Gods of the Ancient Countryside|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2025-45/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}} * Approximately 40 minutes on foot from {{ill|Sotoida Station|ja|外城田駅}} (~3.2 km). * Approximately 42 minutes on foot from {{ill|Tamura Station|ja|田丸駅}} (~3.4 km). * About 10 minutes from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "Tanoya Shrine Tourist Information - Mie Sightseeing" (Accessed November 3, 2013). [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[ja:田乃家神社]] == 関連項目 == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_02.jpg 田乃家神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0705.html 田乃家神社（たのえじんじゃ） 御同座 田乃家御前神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:田乃家神社]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines-qq]] [[Category:田乃家神社]][[ja:田乃家神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:田乃家神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:田乃家神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines-qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 田乃家神社・田乃家御前神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Tanoe-jinja-Tanoemimae-jinja.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]矢野字宮前387 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 36.4 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 36 | 経度秒 = 16.2 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 田乃家神社：大神御滄川神
田乃家御前神社：御前神 |社格= 田乃家神社：式内社（小）、{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}}摂社
田乃家御前神社：皇大神宮摂社 |創建= |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 当藪 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Tanoe Shrine''' (田乃家神社) is a {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrine)|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} (Naikū) within {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. This article also covers the '''Tanoe-Mimae Shrine''' (田乃家御前神社), which shares the same precinct as Tanoe Shrine. Both shrines enshrine deities that serve as {{ill|guardian deities|ja|守護神}} of {{ill|farmhouses|ja|農家}}Gakken Publishing (2013): page 69. [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[ja:田乃家神社]] == Overview == This section describes common information about both shrines. Located at {{ill|Tamaki|ja|玉城町}}, {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Yano Aza Miyamae 387Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 211. There is a theory that the Yano area, where the shrine is situated, is the final resting place of {{ill|Princess Wakatara|ja|稚足姫皇女}}, who served as a {{ill|Saio|ja|斎王}}{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.town.meiwa.mie.jp/romantic/saio/saio4.html|title=Sites Associated with the Saio (Outside of Town) - Meiwa Town Office|publisher=Meiwa Town, Mie Prefecture|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2045-21/www.town.meiwa.mie.jp/romantic/saio/saio4.html|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. According to the "Chūnansei Development Area Archaeological Map," there are four {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mounds) within the precinctsKaneko (1983): p. 61, but field research by Masafumi Kimoto confirmed five. Many {{ill|archaeological sites|ja|遺跡}} from the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} and {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} have been discovered in the Yano area. Additionally, within the precincts are the "Prohibited Killing Stone" established by the {{ill|Kishu Domain|ja|紀州藩}} in {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}} 9 (1724) and a water basin dated {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 13 (1842){{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area04/03.html?iframe=true&width=1000&height=700|title=Tanoya Gozen Shrine - 125 Shrines｜Sengukan|publisher=Sengukan|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2159-39/www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area04/03.html?iframe=true&width=1000&height=700|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}} constructed in the {{ill|shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} architectural style with a wooden shingle roof, facing south and enclosed by a single layer of {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} (sacred fence). There is one {{ill|shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居}}. No {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭箱}} is present. The shrine grounds cover an area of 13,114 m². == Tanoya Shrine == Tanoya Shrine ranks 5th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Inner Shrine (Kōtai Jingū)|ja|皇大神宮}}Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11. It holds a high status among the auxiliary shrines of the Inner ShrineIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 65. Its {{ill|sacred object|ja|神体}} is a {{ill|mirror|ja|鏡}}Kaneko (1983): p. 59. The {{ill|enshrined deity|ja|祭神}} is Ōkami Misamukawa-no-Kami (大神御滄川神). The deity is revered as the guardian of {{ill|farmers|ja|農耕}} and {{ill|households|ja|住宅}}. The prefix "Ōkami" signifies the extension of the divine virtues of {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神}}, the main deity of the Inner Shrine, to this region. The shrine name "Tanoya" has several theories regarding its origin: one suggests it means "on the rice field" (田の上), another links it to the place name "Tanabe" (田邊郷), and a third relates it to "Tato" (田戸), referring to rice fields and residences near government-owned paddiesShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 208. == Tanoya Gozen Shrine == Tanoya Gozen Shrine ranks 6th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine. Its main hall no longer exists, and the deity is now enshrined together with Tanoya ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10. The enshrined deity is Gozen-no-Kami (御前神). Like Ōkami Misamukawa-no-Kami, this deity is also a guardian of farmers. == History == According to the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū Ritual Records|ja|皇大神宮儀式帳}}, the shrine was founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}Kaneko (1983): p. 68. Based on the definition of Ise Jingū's auxiliary shrines, it was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, i.e., before {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. In ancient times, Tanoya Shrine was subject to the {{ill|Shikinen Sengū|ja|式年遷宮}}, rebuilt every 20 years, just like the Inner Shrine. However, after an order to rebuild the shrine in {{ill|Ryakuō|ja|暦応}} 3 (1340), no further records of reconstruction exist, and both the rituals and the shrine site fell into obscurity. The shrine was restored on {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 3, 8th day of the 9th month (October 8, 1663, Gregorian calendar) by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga). == Rituals == Shinto rituals are held in front of the shrine during major festivals such as the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November). == Access == There are two entrance roads to the shrine{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=Course 19 Tamaki Route 2 - Visiting the Gods of the Ancient Countryside|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2025-45/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}} * Approximately 40 minutes on foot from {{ill|Sotoida Station|ja|外城田駅}} (~3.2 km). * Approximately 42 minutes on foot from {{ill|Tamura Station|ja|田丸駅}} (~3.4 km). * About 10 minutes from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "Tanoya Shrine Tourist Information - Mie Sightseeing" (Accessed November 3, 2013). [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[ja:田乃家神社]] == 関連項目 == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_02.jpg 田乃家神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0705.html 田乃家神社（たのえじんじゃ） 御同座 田乃家御前神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:田乃家神社]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines-qq]] [[Category:田乃家神社]][[ja:田乃家神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:田乃家神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:田乃家神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines-qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]]

There is one {{ill|shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居}}. No {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭箱}} is present. The shrine grounds cover an area of 13,114 m². == Tanoya Shrine == Tanoya Shrine ranks 5th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Inner Shrine (Kōtai Jingū)|ja|皇大神宮}}Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11. It holds a high status among the auxiliary shrines of the Inner ShrineIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 65. Its {{ill|sacred object|ja|神体}} is a {{ill|mirror|ja|鏡}}Kaneko (1983): p. 59. The {{ill|enshrined deity|ja|祭神}} is Ōkami Misamukawa-no-Kami (大神御滄川神). The deity is revered as the guardian of {{ill|farmers|ja|農耕}} and {{ill|households|ja|住宅}}. The prefix "Ōkami" signifies the extension of the divine virtues of {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神}}, the main deity of the Inner Shrine, to this region. The shrine name "Tanoya" has several theories regarding its origin: one suggests it means "on the rice field" (田の上), another links it to the place name "Tanabe" (田邊郷), and a third relates it to "Tato" (田戸), referring to rice fields and residences near government-owned paddiesShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 208. == Tanoya Gozen Shrine == Tanoya Gozen Shrine ranks 6th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine. Its main hall no longer exists, and the deity is now enshrined together with Tanoya ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10. The enshrined deity is Gozen-no-Kami (御前神). Like Ōkami Misamukawa-no-Kami, this deity is also a guardian of farmers. == History == According to the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū Ritual Records|ja|皇大神宮儀式帳}}, the shrine was founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}Kaneko (1983): p. 68. Based on the definition of Ise Jingū's auxiliary shrines, it was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, i.e., before {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. In ancient times, Tanoya Shrine was subject to the {{ill|Shikinen Sengū|ja|式年遷宮}}, rebuilt every 20 years, just like the Inner Shrine. However, after an order to rebuild the shrine in {{ill|Ryakuō|ja|暦応}} 3 (1340), no further records of reconstruction exist, and both the rituals and the shrine site fell into obscurity. The shrine was restored on {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 3, 8th day of the 9th month (October 8, 1663, Gregorian calendar) by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga). == Rituals == Shinto rituals are held in front of the shrine during major festivals such as the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November). == Access == There are two entrance roads to the shrine{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=Course 19 Tamaki Route 2 - Visiting the Gods of the Ancient Countryside|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2025-45/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}} * Approximately 40 minutes on foot from {{ill|Sotoida Station|ja|外城田駅}} (~3.2 km). * Approximately 42 minutes on foot from {{ill|Tamura Station|ja|田丸駅}} (~3.4 km). * About 10 minutes from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "Tanoya Shrine Tourist Information - Mie Sightseeing" (Accessed November 3, 2013). [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[ja:田乃家神社]] == 関連項目 == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_02.jpg 田乃家神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0705.html 田乃家神社（たのえじんじゃ） 御同座 田乃家御前神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:田乃家神社]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines-qq]] [[Category:田乃家神社]][[ja:田乃家神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:田乃家神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:田乃家神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines-qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]]

{{神社 |名称= 田乃家神社・田乃家御前神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Tanoe-jinja-Tanoemimae-jinja.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]矢野字宮前387 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 36.4 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 36 | 経度秒 = 16.2 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 田乃家神社：大神御滄川神
田乃家御前神社：御前神 |社格= 田乃家神社：式内社（小）、{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}}摂社
田乃家御前神社：皇大神宮摂社 |創建= |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 当藪 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Tanoe Shrine''' (田乃家神社) is a {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrine)|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} (Naikū) within {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. This article also covers the '''Tanoe-Mimae Shrine''' (田乃家御前神社), which shares the same precinct as Tanoe Shrine. Both shrines enshrine deities that serve as {{ill|guardian deities|ja|守護神}} of {{ill|farmhouses|ja|農家}}Gakken Publishing (2013): page 69. [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[ja:田乃家神社]] == Overview == This section describes common information about both shrines. Located at {{ill|Tamaki|ja|玉城町}}, {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Yano Aza Miyamae 387Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 211. There is a theory that the Yano area, where the shrine is situated, is the final resting place of {{ill|Princess Wakatara|ja|稚足姫皇女}}, who served as a {{ill|Saio|ja|斎王}}{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.town.meiwa.mie.jp/romantic/saio/saio4.html|title=Sites Associated with the Saio (Outside of Town) - Meiwa Town Office|publisher=Meiwa Town, Mie Prefecture|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2045-21/www.town.meiwa.mie.jp/romantic/saio/saio4.html|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. According to the "Chūnansei Development Area Archaeological Map," there are four {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mounds) within the precinctsKaneko (1983): p. 61, but field research by Masafumi Kimoto confirmed five. Many {{ill|archaeological sites|ja|遺跡}} from the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} and {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} have been discovered in the Yano area. Additionally, within the precincts are the "Prohibited Killing Stone" established by the {{ill|Kishu Domain|ja|紀州藩}} in {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}} 9 (1724) and a water basin dated {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 13 (1842){{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area04/03.html?iframe=true&width=1000&height=700|title=Tanoya Gozen Shrine - 125 Shrines｜Sengukan|publisher=Sengukan|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2159-39/www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area04/03.html?iframe=true&width=1000&height=700|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}} constructed in the {{ill|shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} architectural style with a wooden shingle roof, facing south and enclosed by a single layer of {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} (sacred fence). There is one {{ill|shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居}}. No {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭箱}} is present. The shrine grounds cover an area of 13,114 m². == Tanoya Shrine == Tanoya Shrine ranks 5th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Inner Shrine (Kōtai Jingū)|ja|皇大神宮}}Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11. It holds a high status among the auxiliary shrines of the Inner ShrineIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 65. Its {{ill|sacred object|ja|神体}} is a {{ill|mirror|ja|鏡}}Kaneko (1983): p. 59. The {{ill|enshrined deity|ja|祭神}} is Ōkami Misamukawa-no-Kami (大神御滄川神). The deity is revered as the guardian of {{ill|farmers|ja|農耕}} and {{ill|households|ja|住宅}}. The prefix "Ōkami" signifies the extension of the divine virtues of {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神}}, the main deity of the Inner Shrine, to this region. The shrine name "Tanoya" has several theories regarding its origin: one suggests it means "on the rice field" (田の上), another links it to the place name "Tanabe" (田邊郷), and a third relates it to "Tato" (田戸), referring to rice fields and residences near government-owned paddiesShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 208. == Tanoya Gozen Shrine == Tanoya Gozen Shrine ranks 6th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine. Its main hall no longer exists, and the deity is now enshrined together with Tanoya ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10. The enshrined deity is Gozen-no-Kami (御前神). Like Ōkami Misamukawa-no-Kami, this deity is also a guardian of farmers. == History == According to the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū Ritual Records|ja|皇大神宮儀式帳}}, the shrine was founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}Kaneko (1983): p. 68. Based on the definition of Ise Jingū's auxiliary shrines, it was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, i.e., before {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. In ancient times, Tanoya Shrine was subject to the {{ill|Shikinen Sengū|ja|式年遷宮}}, rebuilt every 20 years, just like the Inner Shrine. However, after an order to rebuild the shrine in {{ill|Ryakuō|ja|暦応}} 3 (1340), no further records of reconstruction exist, and both the rituals and the shrine site fell into obscurity. The shrine was restored on {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 3, 8th day of the 9th month (October 8, 1663, Gregorian calendar) by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga). == Rituals == Shinto rituals are held in front of the shrine during major festivals such as the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November). == Access == There are two entrance roads to the shrine{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=Course 19 Tamaki Route 2 - Visiting the Gods of the Ancient Countryside|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2025-45/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}} * Approximately 40 minutes on foot from {{ill|Sotoida Station|ja|外城田駅}} (~3.2 km). * Approximately 42 minutes on foot from {{ill|Tamura Station|ja|田丸駅}} (~3.4 km). * About 10 minutes from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "Tanoya Shrine Tourist Information - Mie Sightseeing" (Accessed November 3, 2013). [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[ja:田乃家神社]] == 関連項目 == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_02.jpg 田乃家神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0705.html 田乃家神社（たのえじんじゃ） 御同座 田乃家御前神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:田乃家神社]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines-qq]] [[Category:田乃家神社]][[ja:田乃家神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:田乃家神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:田乃家神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines-qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]] [[ja:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]]

However, after an order to rebuild the shrine in {{ill|Ryakuō|ja|暦応}} 3 (1340), no further records of reconstruction exist, and both the rituals and the shrine site fell into obscurity. The shrine was restored on {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 3, 8th day of the 9th month (October 8, 1663, Gregorian calendar) by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga). == Rituals == Shinto rituals are held in front of the shrine during major festivals such as the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November). == Access == There are two entrance roads to the shrine{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=Course 19 Tamaki Route 2 - Visiting the Gods of the Ancient Countryside|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2025-45/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}} * Approximately 40 minutes on foot from {{ill|Sotoida Station|ja|外城田駅}} (~3.2 km). * Approximately 42 minutes on foot from {{ill|Tamura Station|ja|田丸駅}} (~3.4 km). * About 10 minutes from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "Tanoya Shrine Tourist Information - Mie Sightseeing" (Accessed November 3, 2013). [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[ja:田乃家神社]] == 関連項目 == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_02.jpg 田乃家神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0705.html 田乃家神社（たのえじんじゃ） 御同座 田乃家御前神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:田乃家神社]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines-qq]] [[Category:田乃家神社]][[ja:田乃家神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:田乃家神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:田乃家神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines-qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:11]] [[en:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]] [[ja:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 田乃家神社・田乃家御前神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Tanoe-jinja-Tanoemimae-jinja.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]矢野字宮前387 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 36.4 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 36 | 経度秒 = 16.2 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 田乃家神社：大神御滄川神
田乃家御前神社：御前神 |社格= 田乃家神社：式内社（小）、{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}}摂社
田乃家御前神社：皇大神宮摂社 |創建= |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 当藪 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Tanoe Shrine''' (田乃家神社) is a {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrine)|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} (Naikū) within {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. This article also covers the '''Tanoe-Mimae Shrine''' (田乃家御前神社), which shares the same precinct as Tanoe Shrine. Both shrines enshrine deities that serve as {{ill|guardian deities|ja|守護神}} of {{ill|farmhouses|ja|農家}}Gakken Publishing (2013): page 69. [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[ja:田乃家神社]] == Overview == This section describes common information about both shrines. Located at {{ill|Tamaki|ja|玉城町}}, {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Yano Aza Miyamae 387Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 211. There is a theory that the Yano area, where the shrine is situated, is the final resting place of {{ill|Princess Wakatara|ja|稚足姫皇女}}, who served as a {{ill|Saio|ja|斎王}}{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.town.meiwa.mie.jp/romantic/saio/saio4.html|title=Sites Associated with the Saio (Outside of Town) - Meiwa Town Office|publisher=Meiwa Town, Mie Prefecture|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2045-21/www.town.meiwa.mie.jp/romantic/saio/saio4.html|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. According to the "Chūnansei Development Area Archaeological Map," there are four {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mounds) within the precinctsKaneko (1983): p. 61, but field research by Masafumi Kimoto confirmed five. Many {{ill|archaeological sites|ja|遺跡}} from the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} and {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} have been discovered in the Yano area. Additionally, within the precincts are the "Prohibited Killing Stone" established by the {{ill|Kishu Domain|ja|紀州藩}} in {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}} 9 (1724) and a water basin dated {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 13 (1842){{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area04/03.html?iframe=true&width=1000&height=700|title=Tanoya Gozen Shrine - 125 Shrines｜Sengukan|publisher=Sengukan|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2159-39/www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area04/03.html?iframe=true&width=1000&height=700|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}} constructed in the {{ill|shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} architectural style with a wooden shingle roof, facing south and enclosed by a single layer of {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} (sacred fence). There is one {{ill|shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居}}. No {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭箱}} is present. The shrine grounds cover an area of 13,114 m². == Tanoya Shrine == Tanoya Shrine ranks 5th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Inner Shrine (Kōtai Jingū)|ja|皇大神宮}}Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11. It holds a high status among the auxiliary shrines of the Inner ShrineIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 65. Its {{ill|sacred object|ja|神体}} is a {{ill|mirror|ja|鏡}}Kaneko (1983): p. 59. The {{ill|enshrined deity|ja|祭神}} is Ōkami Misamukawa-no-Kami (大神御滄川神). The deity is revered as the guardian of {{ill|farmers|ja|農耕}} and {{ill|households|ja|住宅}}. The prefix "Ōkami" signifies the extension of the divine virtues of {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神}}, the main deity of the Inner Shrine, to this region. The shrine name "Tanoya" has several theories regarding its origin: one suggests it means "on the rice field" (田の上), another links it to the place name "Tanabe" (田邊郷), and a third relates it to "Tato" (田戸), referring to rice fields and residences near government-owned paddiesShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 208. == Tanoya Gozen Shrine == Tanoya Gozen Shrine ranks 6th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine. Its main hall no longer exists, and the deity is now enshrined together with Tanoya ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10. The enshrined deity is Gozen-no-Kami (御前神). Like Ōkami Misamukawa-no-Kami, this deity is also a guardian of farmers. == History == According to the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū Ritual Records|ja|皇大神宮儀式帳}}, the shrine was founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}Kaneko (1983): p. 68. Based on the definition of Ise Jingū's auxiliary shrines, it was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, i.e., before {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. In ancient times, Tanoya Shrine was subject to the {{ill|Shikinen Sengū|ja|式年遷宮}}, rebuilt every 20 years, just like the Inner Shrine. However, after an order to rebuild the shrine in {{ill|Ryakuō|ja|暦応}} 3 (1340), no further records of reconstruction exist, and both the rituals and the shrine site fell into obscurity. The shrine was restored on {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 3, 8th day of the 9th month (October 8, 1663, Gregorian calendar) by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga). == Rituals == Shinto rituals are held in front of the shrine during major festivals such as the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November). == Access == There are two entrance roads to the shrine{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=Course 19 Tamaki Route 2 - Visiting the Gods of the Ancient Countryside|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2025-45/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}} * Approximately 40 minutes on foot from {{ill|Sotoida Station|ja|外城田駅}} (~3.2 km). * Approximately 42 minutes on foot from {{ill|Tamura Station|ja|田丸駅}} (~3.4 km). * About 10 minutes from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "Tanoya Shrine Tourist Information - Mie Sightseeing" (Accessed November 3, 2013). [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[ja:田乃家神社]] == 関連項目 == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_02.jpg 田乃家神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0705.html 田乃家神社（たのえじんじゃ） 御同座 田乃家御前神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:田乃家神社]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines-qq]] [[Category:田乃家神社]][[ja:田乃家神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:田乃家神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:田乃家神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines-qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:11]] [[en:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]] [[ja:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]]

However, after an order to rebuild the shrine in {{ill|Ryakuō|ja|暦応}} 3 (1340), no further records of reconstruction exist, and both the rituals and the shrine site fell into obscurity. The shrine was restored on {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 3, 8th day of the 9th month (October 8, 1663, Gregorian calendar) by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga). == Rituals == Shinto rituals are held in front of the shrine during major festivals such as the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November). == Access == There are two entrance roads to the shrine{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=Course 19 Tamaki Route 2 - Visiting the Gods of the Ancient Countryside|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2025-45/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}} * Approximately 40 minutes on foot from {{ill|Sotoida Station|ja|外城田駅}} (~3.2 km). * Approximately 42 minutes on foot from {{ill|Tamura Station|ja|田丸駅}} (~3.4 km). * About 10 minutes from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "Tanoya Shrine Tourist Information - Mie Sightseeing" (Accessed November 3, 2013). [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[ja:田乃家神社]] == 関連項目 == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_02.jpg 田乃家神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0705.html 田乃家神社（たのえじんじゃ） 御同座 田乃家御前神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:田乃家神社]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines-qq]] [[Category:田乃家神社]][[ja:田乃家神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:田乃家神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:田乃家神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines-qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[en:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]] [[ja:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 田乃家神社・田乃家御前神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Tanoe-jinja-Tanoemimae-jinja.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]矢野字宮前387 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 36.4 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 36 | 経度秒 = 16.2 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 田乃家神社：大神御滄川神
田乃家御前神社：御前神 |社格= 田乃家神社：式内社（小）、{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}}摂社
田乃家御前神社：皇大神宮摂社 |創建= |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 当藪 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Tanoe Shrine''' (田乃家神社) is a {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrine)|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} (Naikū) within {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. This article also covers the '''Tanoe-Mimae Shrine''' (田乃家御前神社), which shares the same precinct as Tanoe Shrine. Both shrines enshrine deities that serve as {{ill|guardian deities|ja|守護神}} of {{ill|farmhouses|ja|農家}}Gakken Publishing (2013): page 69. [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[ja:田乃家神社]] == Overview == This section describes common information about both shrines. Located at {{ill|Tamaki|ja|玉城町}}, {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Yano Aza Miyamae 387Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 211. There is a theory that the Yano area, where the shrine is situated, is the final resting place of {{ill|Princess Wakatara|ja|稚足姫皇女}}, who served as a {{ill|Saio|ja|斎王}}{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.town.meiwa.mie.jp/romantic/saio/saio4.html|title=Sites Associated with the Saio (Outside of Town) - Meiwa Town Office|publisher=Meiwa Town, Mie Prefecture|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2045-21/www.town.meiwa.mie.jp/romantic/saio/saio4.html|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. According to the "Chūnansei Development Area Archaeological Map," there are four {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mounds) within the precinctsKaneko (1983): p. 61, but field research by Masafumi Kimoto confirmed five. Many {{ill|archaeological sites|ja|遺跡}} from the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} and {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} have been discovered in the Yano area. Additionally, within the precincts are the "Prohibited Killing Stone" established by the {{ill|Kishu Domain|ja|紀州藩}} in {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}} 9 (1724) and a water basin dated {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 13 (1842){{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area04/03.html?iframe=true&width=1000&height=700|title=Tanoya Gozen Shrine - 125 Shrines｜Sengukan|publisher=Sengukan|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2159-39/www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area04/03.html?iframe=true&width=1000&height=700|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}} constructed in the {{ill|shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} architectural style with a wooden shingle roof, facing south and enclosed by a single layer of {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} (sacred fence). There is one {{ill|shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居}}. No {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭箱}} is present. The shrine grounds cover an area of 13,114 m². == Tanoya Shrine == Tanoya Shrine ranks 5th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Inner Shrine (Kōtai Jingū)|ja|皇大神宮}}Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11. It holds a high status among the auxiliary shrines of the Inner ShrineIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 65. Its {{ill|sacred object|ja|神体}} is a {{ill|mirror|ja|鏡}}Kaneko (1983): p. 59. The {{ill|enshrined deity|ja|祭神}} is Ōkami Misamukawa-no-Kami (大神御滄川神). The deity is revered as the guardian of {{ill|farmers|ja|農耕}} and {{ill|households|ja|住宅}}. The prefix "Ōkami" signifies the extension of the divine virtues of {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神}}, the main deity of the Inner Shrine, to this region. The shrine name "Tanoya" has several theories regarding its origin: one suggests it means "on the rice field" (田の上), another links it to the place name "Tanabe" (田邊郷), and a third relates it to "Tato" (田戸), referring to rice fields and residences near government-owned paddiesShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 208. == Tanoya Gozen Shrine == Tanoya Gozen Shrine ranks 6th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine. Its main hall no longer exists, and the deity is now enshrined together with Tanoya ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10. The enshrined deity is Gozen-no-Kami (御前神). Like Ōkami Misamukawa-no-Kami, this deity is also a guardian of farmers. == History == According to the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū Ritual Records|ja|皇大神宮儀式帳}}, the shrine was founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}Kaneko (1983): p. 68. Based on the definition of Ise Jingū's auxiliary shrines, it was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, i.e., before {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. In ancient times, Tanoya Shrine was subject to the {{ill|Shikinen Sengū|ja|式年遷宮}}, rebuilt every 20 years, just like the Inner Shrine. However, after an order to rebuild the shrine in {{ill|Ryakuō|ja|暦応}} 3 (1340), no further records of reconstruction exist, and both the rituals and the shrine site fell into obscurity. The shrine was restored on {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 3, 8th day of the 9th month (October 8, 1663, Gregorian calendar) by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga). == Rituals == Shinto rituals are held in front of the shrine during major festivals such as the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November). == Access == There are two entrance roads to the shrine{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=Course 19 Tamaki Route 2 - Visiting the Gods of the Ancient Countryside|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2025-45/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}} * Approximately 40 minutes on foot from {{ill|Sotoida Station|ja|外城田駅}} (~3.2 km). * Approximately 42 minutes on foot from {{ill|Tamura Station|ja|田丸駅}} (~3.4 km). * About 10 minutes from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "Tanoya Shrine Tourist Information - Mie Sightseeing" (Accessed November 3, 2013). [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[ja:田乃家神社]] == 関連項目 == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_02.jpg 田乃家神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0705.html 田乃家神社（たのえじんじゃ） 御同座 田乃家御前神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:田乃家神社]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines-qq]] [[Category:田乃家神社]][[ja:田乃家神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:田乃家神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:田乃家神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines-qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[en:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]] [[ja:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]]

{{神社 |名称= 田乃家神社・田乃家御前神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Tanoe-jinja-Tanoemimae-jinja.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]矢野字宮前387 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 36.4 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 36 | 経度秒 = 16.2 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 田乃家神社：大神御滄川神
田乃家御前神社：御前神 |社格= 田乃家神社：式内社（小）、{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}}摂社
田乃家御前神社：皇大神宮摂社 |創建= |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 当藪 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Tanoe Shrine''' (田乃家神社) is a {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrine)|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} (Naikū) within {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}.
