Both shrines enshrine deities that serve as {{ill|guardian deities|ja|守護神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|farmhouses|ja|農家|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Gakken Publishing (2013): page 69. == Overview == This section describes common information about both shrines. Located at {{ill|Tamaki|ja|玉城町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Yano Aza Miyamae 387Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 211. There is a theory that the Yano area, where the shrine is situated, is the final resting place of {{ill|Princess Wakatara|ja|稚足姫皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who served as a {{ill|Saio|ja|斎王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.town.meiwa.mie.jp/romantic/saio/saio4.html|title=Sites Associated with the Saio (Outside of Town) - Meiwa Town Office|publisher=Meiwa Town, Mie Prefecture|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2045-21/www.town.meiwa.mie.jp/romantic/saio/saio4.html|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. According to the "Chūnansei Development Area Archaeological Map," there are four {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (ancient Kofuns) within the precinctsKaneko (1983): p. 61, but field research by Masafumi Kimoto confirmed five. Many {{ill|archaeological sites|ja|遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} have been discovered in the Yano area. Additionally, within the precincts are the "Prohibited Killing Stone" established by the {{ill|Kishu Domain|ja|紀州藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 9 (1724) and a water basin dated {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 13 (1842){{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area04/03.html?iframe=true&width=1000&height=700|title=Tanoya Gozen Shrine - 125 Shrines｜Sengukan|publisher=Sengukan|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2159-39/www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area04/03.html?iframe=true&width=1000&height=700|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} constructed in the {{ill|shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} architectural style with a wooden shingle roof, facing south and enclosed by a single layer of {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (sacred fence). There is one {{ill|shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. No {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is present. The shrine grounds cover an area of 13,114 m². == Tanoya Shrine == Tanoya Shrine Ranks 5th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Inner Shrine (Kōtai Jingū)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11. It holds a high status among the auxiliary shrines of the Inner ShrineIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 65. Its {{ill|sacred object|ja|神体|1=Shintai|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is a {{ill|mirror|ja|鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Kaneko (1983): p. 59. The {{ill|enshrined deity|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is Ōkami Misamukawa-no-Kami (大神御滄川神). The deity is revered as the guardian of {{ill|farmers|ja|農耕|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|households|ja|住宅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The prefix "Ōkami" signifies the extension of the divine virtues of {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the main deity of the Inner Shrine, to this region. The shrine name "Tanoya" has several theories regarding its origin: one suggests it means "on the rice field" (田の上), another links it to the place name "Tanabe" (田邊郷), and a third relates it to "Tato" (田戸), referring to rice fields and residences near government-owned paddiesShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 208. == Tanoya Gozen Shrine == Tanoya Gozen Shrine Ranks 6th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine. Its main hall no longer exists, and the deity is now enshrined together with Tanoya ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10. The enshrined deity is Gozen-no-Kami (御前神). Like Ōkami Misamukawa-no-Kami, this deity is also a guardian of farmers. == History == According to the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū Ritual Records|ja|皇大神宮儀式帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Kaneko (1983): p. 68. Based on the definition of Ise Jingū's auxiliary shrines, it was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, i.e., before {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. In ancient times, Tanoya Shrine was subject to the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|lt=Shikinen Sengū|ja|式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, rebuilt every 20 years, just like the Inner Shrine. However, after an order to rebuild the shrine in {{ill|Ryakuō|ja|暦応|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3 (1340), no further records of reconstruction exist, and both the rituals and the shrine site fell into obscurity. The shrine was restored on {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3, 8th day of the 9th month (October 8, 1663, Gregorian calendar) by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga). == Rituals == Shinto rituals are held in front of the shrine during major festivals such as the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (November). == Access == There are two entrance roads to the shrine{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=Course 19 Tamaki Route 2 - Visiting the Gods of the Ancient Countryside|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2025-45/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ** Approximately 40 minutes on foot from {{ill|Sotoida Station|ja|外城田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (~3.2 km). ** Approximately 42 minutes on foot from {{ill|Tamura Station|ja|田丸駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (~3.4 km). * About 10 minutes from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "Tanoya Shrine Tourist Information - Mie Sightseeing" (Accessed November 3, 2013). == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_02.jpg 田乃家神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0705.html 田乃家神社（たのえじんじゃ） 御同座 田乃家御前神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|田乃家神社|version=98200648|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:田乃家神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] }} [[ja:田乃家神社]] [[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]] [[sn:anoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]] [[en:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]]

Both shrines enshrine deities that serve as {{ill|guardian deities|ja|守護神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deities|qq=}} of {{ill|farmhouses|ja|農家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/farmhouses|qq=}}Gakken Publishing (2013): page 69. == Overview == This section describes common information about both shrines. Located at {{ill|Tamaki|ja|玉城町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki|qq=}}, {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai District|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}, Yano Aza Miyamae 387Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 211. There is a theory that the Yano area, where the shrine is situated, is the final resting place of {{ill|Princess Wakatara|ja|稚足姫皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Wakatara|qq=}}, who served as a {{ill|Saio|ja|斎王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saio|qq=}}{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.town.meiwa.mie.jp/romantic/saio/saio4.html|title=Sites Associated with the Saio (Outside of Town) - Meiwa Town Office|publisher=Meiwa Town, Mie Prefecture|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2045-21/www.town.meiwa.mie.jp/romantic/saio/saio4.html|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. According to the "Chūnansei Development Area Archaeological Map," there are four {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} (ancient Kofuns) within the precinctsKaneko (1983): p. 61, but field research by Masafumi Kimoto confirmed five. Many {{ill|archaeological sites|ja|遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/archaeological sites|qq=}} from the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yayoi period|qq=}} and {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} have been discovered in the Yano area. Additionally, within the precincts are the "Prohibited Killing Stone" established by the {{ill|Kishu Domain|ja|紀州藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishu Domain|qq=}} in {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōhō|qq=}} 9 (1724) and a water basin dated {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpō|qq=}} 13 (1842){{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area04/03.html?iframe=true&width=1000&height=700|title=Tanoya Gozen Shrine - 125 Shrines｜Sengukan|publisher=Sengukan|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2159-39/www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area04/03.html?iframe=true&width=1000&height=700|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|qq=}} constructed in the {{ill|shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shinmei-zukuri|qq=}} architectural style with a wooden shingle roof, facing south and enclosed by a single layer of {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tamagaki|qq=}} (sacred fence). There is one {{ill|shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shinmei torii|qq=}}. No {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offertory box|qq=}} is present. The shrine grounds cover an area of 13,114 m². == Tanoya Shrine == Tanoya Shrine Ranks 5th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Inner Shrine (Kōtai Jingū)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine (Kōtai Jingū)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}}Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11. It holds a high status among the auxiliary shrines of the Inner ShrineIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 65. Its {{ill|sacred object|ja|神体|1=Shintai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred object|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintai|qq=}} is a {{ill|mirror|ja|鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mirror|qq=}}Kaneko (1983): p. 59. The {{ill|enshrined deity|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|qq=}} is Ōkami Misamukawa-no-Kami (大神御滄川神). The deity is revered as the guardian of {{ill|farmers|ja|農耕|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/farmers|qq=}} and {{ill|households|ja|住宅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/households|qq=}}. The prefix "Ōkami" signifies the extension of the divine virtues of {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}, the main deity of the Inner Shrine, to this region. The shrine name "Tanoya" has several theories regarding its origin: one suggests it means "on the rice field" (田の上), another links it to the place name "Tanabe" (田邊郷), and a third relates it to "Tato" (田戸), referring to rice fields and residences near government-owned paddiesShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 208. == Tanoya Gozen Shrine == Tanoya Gozen Shrine Ranks 6th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine. Its main hall no longer exists, and the deity is now enshrined together with Tanoya ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10. The enshrined deity is Gozen-no-Kami (御前神). Like Ōkami Misamukawa-no-Kami, this deity is also a guardian of farmers. == History == According to the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū Ritual Records|ja|皇大神宮儀式帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū Ritual Records|qq=}}, the shrine was founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Yūryaku|qq=}}Kaneko (1983): p. 68. Based on the definition of Ise Jingū's auxiliary shrines, it was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, i.e., before {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|qq=}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. In ancient times, Tanoya Shrine was subject to the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|lt=Shikinen Sengū|ja|式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}}, rebuilt every 20 years, just like the Inner Shrine. However, after an order to rebuild the shrine in {{ill|Ryakuō|ja|暦応|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryakuō|qq=}} 3 (1340), no further records of reconstruction exist, and both the rituals and the shrine site fell into obscurity. The shrine was restored on {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbun|qq=}} 3, 8th day of the 9th month (October 8, 1663, Gregorian calendar) by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|qq=}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga). == Rituals == Shinto rituals are held in front of the shrine during major festivals such as the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinensai|qq=}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niinamesai|qq=}} (November). == Access == There are two entrance roads to the shrine{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=Course 19 Tamaki Route 2 - Visiting the Gods of the Ancient Countryside|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2025-45/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Central|qq=}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangu Line|qq=}} ** Approximately 40 minutes on foot from {{ill|Sotoida Station|ja|外城田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotoida Station|qq=}} (~3.2 km). ** Approximately 42 minutes on foot from {{ill|Tamura Station|ja|田丸駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamura Station|qq=}} (~3.4 km). * About 10 minutes from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki Interchange|qq=}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Expressway|qq=}}Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "Tanoya Shrine Tourist Information - Mie Sightseeing" (Accessed November 3, 2013). == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_02.jpg 田乃家神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0705.html 田乃家神社（たのえじんじゃ） 御同座 田乃家御前神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|田乃家神社|version=98200648|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] }} [[ja:田乃家神社]] [[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]] [[sn:anoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]] [[en:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Engishiki Jinmyocho" to "Engishiki Jinmyōchō"
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 田乃家神社・田乃家御前神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Tanoe-jinja-Tanoemimae-jinja.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]矢野字宮前387 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 36.4 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 36 | 経度秒 = 16.2 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 田乃家神社：大神御滄川神
田乃家御前神社：御前神 |社格= 田乃家神社：式内社（小）、{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}}摂社
田乃家御前神社：皇大神宮摂社 |創建= |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 当藪 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Tanoe Shrine''' (田乃家神社) is a {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrine)|ja|摂末社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sessha (auxiliary shrine)|qq=}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (Naikū) within {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}}. This article also covers the '''Tanoe-Mimae Shrine''' (田乃家御前神社), which shares the same precinct as Tanoe Shrine. Both shrines enshrine deities that serve as {{ill|guardian deities|ja|守護神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deities|qq=}} of {{ill|farmhouses|ja|農家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/farmhouses|qq=}}Gakken Publishing (2013): page 69. == Overview == This section describes common information about both shrines. Located at {{ill|Tamaki|ja|玉城町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki|qq=}}, {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai District|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}, Yano Aza Miyamae 387Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 211. There is a theory that the Yano area, where the shrine is situated, is the final resting place of {{ill|Princess Wakatara|ja|稚足姫皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Wakatara|qq=}}, who served as a {{ill|Saio|ja|斎王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saio|qq=}}{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.town.meiwa.mie.jp/romantic/saio/saio4.html|title=Sites Associated with the Saio (Outside of Town) - Meiwa Town Office|publisher=Meiwa Town, Mie Prefecture|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2045-21/www.town.meiwa.mie.jp/romantic/saio/saio4.html|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. According to the "Chūnansei Development Area Archaeological Map," there are four {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} (ancient Kofuns) within the precinctsKaneko (1983): p. 61, but field research by Masafumi Kimoto confirmed five. Many {{ill|archaeological sites|ja|遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/archaeological sites|qq=}} from the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yayoi period|qq=}} and {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} have been discovered in the Yano area. Additionally, within the precincts are the "Prohibited Killing Stone" established by the {{ill|Kishu Domain|ja|紀州藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishu Domain|qq=}} in {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōhō|qq=}} 9 (1724) and a water basin dated {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpō|qq=}} 13 (1842){{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area04/03.html?iframe=true&width=1000&height=700|title=Tanoya Gozen Shrine - 125 Shrines｜Sengukan|publisher=Sengukan|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2159-39/www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area04/03.html?iframe=true&width=1000&height=700|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|qq=}} constructed in the {{ill|shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shinmei-zukuri|qq=}} architectural style with a wooden shingle roof, facing south and enclosed by a single layer of {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tamagaki|qq=}} (sacred fence). There is one {{ill|shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shinmei torii|qq=}}. No {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offertory box|qq=}} is present. The shrine grounds cover an area of 13,114 m². == Tanoya Shrine == Tanoya Shrine Ranks 5th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Inner Shrine (Kōtai Jingū)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine (Kōtai Jingū)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}}Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11. It holds a high status among the auxiliary shrines of the Inner ShrineIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 65. Its {{ill|sacred object|ja|神体|1=Shintai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred object|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintai|qq=}} is a {{ill|mirror|ja|鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mirror|qq=}}Kaneko (1983): p. 59. The {{ill|enshrined deity|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|qq=}} is Ōkami Misamukawa-no-Kami (大神御滄川神). The deity is revered as the guardian of {{ill|farmers|ja|農耕|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/farmers|qq=}} and {{ill|households|ja|住宅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/households|qq=}}. The prefix "Ōkami" signifies the extension of the divine virtues of {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}, the main deity of the Inner Shrine, to this region. The shrine name "Tanoya" has several theories regarding its origin: one suggests it means "on the rice field" (田の上), another links it to the place name "Tanabe" (田邊郷), and a third relates it to "Tato" (田戸), referring to rice fields and residences near government-owned paddiesShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 208. == Tanoya Gozen Shrine == Tanoya Gozen Shrine Ranks 6th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine. Its main hall no longer exists, and the deity is now enshrined together with Tanoya ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10. The enshrined deity is Gozen-no-Kami (御前神). Like Ōkami Misamukawa-no-Kami, this deity is also a guardian of farmers. == History == According to the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū Ritual Records|ja|皇大神宮儀式帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū Ritual Records|qq=}}, the shrine was founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Yūryaku|qq=}}Kaneko (1983): p. 68. Based on the definition of Ise Jingū's auxiliary shrines, it was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, i.e., before {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|qq=}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. In ancient times, Tanoya Shrine was subject to the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|lt=Shikinen Sengū|ja|式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}}, rebuilt every 20 years, just like the Inner Shrine. However, after an order to rebuild the shrine in {{ill|Ryakuō|ja|暦応|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryakuō|qq=}} 3 (1340), no further records of reconstruction exist, and both the rituals and the shrine site fell into obscurity. The shrine was restored on {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbun|qq=}} 3, 8th day of the 9th month (October 8, 1663, Gregorian calendar) by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|qq=}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga). == Rituals == Shinto rituals are held in front of the shrine during major festivals such as the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinensai|qq=}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niinamesai|qq=}} (November). == Access == There are two entrance roads to the shrine{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=Course 19 Tamaki Route 2 - Visiting the Gods of the Ancient Countryside|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2025-45/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Central|qq=}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangu Line|qq=}} ** Approximately 40 minutes on foot from {{ill|Sotoida Station|ja|外城田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotoida Station|qq=}} (~3.2 km). ** Approximately 42 minutes on foot from {{ill|Tamura Station|ja|田丸駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamura Station|qq=}} (~3.4 km). * About 10 minutes from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki Interchange|qq=}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Expressway|qq=}}Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "Tanoya Shrine Tourist Information - Mie Sightseeing" (Accessed November 3, 2013). == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_02.jpg 田乃家神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0705.html 田乃家神社（たのえじんじゃ） 御同座 田乃家御前神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|田乃家神社|version=98200648|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] }} [[ja:田乃家神社]] [[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]] [[sn:anoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]] [[en:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]]

revision.comment: Bot: fix redirect [[Kannamesai]] → [[Kannamesai Festival]]
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 田乃家神社・田乃家御前神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Tanoe-jinja-Tanoemimae-jinja.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]矢野字宮前387 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 36.4 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 36 | 経度秒 = 16.2 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 田乃家神社：大神御滄川神
田乃家御前神社：御前神 |社格= 田乃家神社：式内社（小）、{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}}摂社
田乃家御前神社：皇大神宮摂社 |創建= |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 当藪 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Tanoe Shrine''' (田乃家神社) is a {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrine)|ja|摂末社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sessha (auxiliary shrine)|qq=}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (Naikū) within {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}}. This article also covers the '''Tanoe-Mimae Shrine''' (田乃家御前神社), which shares the same precinct as Tanoe Shrine. Both shrines enshrine deities that serve as {{ill|guardian deities|ja|守護神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deities|qq=}} of {{ill|farmhouses|ja|農家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/farmhouses|qq=}}Gakken Publishing (2013): page 69. == Overview == This section describes common information about both shrines. Located at {{ill|Tamaki|ja|玉城町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki|qq=}}, {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai District|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}, Yano Aza Miyamae 387Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 211. There is a theory that the Yano area, where the shrine is situated, is the final resting place of {{ill|Princess Wakatara|ja|稚足姫皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Wakatara|qq=}}, who served as a {{ill|Saio|ja|斎王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saio|qq=}}{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.town.meiwa.mie.jp/romantic/saio/saio4.html|title=Sites Associated with the Saio (Outside of Town) - Meiwa Town Office|publisher=Meiwa Town, Mie Prefecture|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2045-21/www.town.meiwa.mie.jp/romantic/saio/saio4.html|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. According to the "Chūnansei Development Area Archaeological Map," there are four {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} (ancient Kofuns) within the precinctsKaneko (1983): p. 61, but field research by Masafumi Kimoto confirmed five. Many {{ill|archaeological sites|ja|遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/archaeological sites|qq=}} from the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yayoi period|qq=}} and {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} have been discovered in the Yano area. Additionally, within the precincts are the "Prohibited Killing Stone" established by the {{ill|Kishu Domain|ja|紀州藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishu Domain|qq=}} in {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōhō|qq=}} 9 (1724) and a water basin dated {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpō|qq=}} 13 (1842){{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area04/03.html?iframe=true&width=1000&height=700|title=Tanoya Gozen Shrine - 125 Shrines｜Sengukan|publisher=Sengukan|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2159-39/www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area04/03.html?iframe=true&width=1000&height=700|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|qq=}} constructed in the {{ill|shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shinmei-zukuri|qq=}} architectural style with a wooden shingle roof, facing south and enclosed by a single layer of {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tamagaki|qq=}} (sacred fence). There is one {{ill|shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shinmei torii|qq=}}. No {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offertory box|qq=}} is present. The shrine grounds cover an area of 13,114 m². == Tanoya Shrine == Tanoya Shrine Ranks 5th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Inner Shrine (Kōtai Jingū)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine (Kōtai Jingū)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}}Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11. It holds a high status among the auxiliary shrines of the Inner ShrineIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 65. Its {{ill|sacred object|ja|神体|1=Shintai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred object|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintai|qq=}} is a {{ill|mirror|ja|鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mirror|qq=}}Kaneko (1983): p. 59. The {{ill|enshrined deity|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|qq=}} is Ōkami Misamukawa-no-Kami (大神御滄川神). The deity is revered as the guardian of {{ill|farmers|ja|農耕|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/farmers|qq=}} and {{ill|households|ja|住宅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/households|qq=}}. The prefix "Ōkami" signifies the extension of the divine virtues of {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}, the main deity of the Inner Shrine, to this region. The shrine name "Tanoya" has several theories regarding its origin: one suggests it means "on the rice field" (田の上), another links it to the place name "Tanabe" (田邊郷), and a third relates it to "Tato" (田戸), referring to rice fields and residences near government-owned paddiesShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 208. == Tanoya Gozen Shrine == Tanoya Gozen Shrine Ranks 6th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine. Its main hall no longer exists, and the deity is now enshrined together with Tanoya ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10. The enshrined deity is Gozen-no-Kami (御前神). Like Ōkami Misamukawa-no-Kami, this deity is also a guardian of farmers. == History == According to the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū Ritual Records|ja|皇大神宮儀式帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū Ritual Records|qq=}}, the shrine was founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Yūryaku|qq=}}Kaneko (1983): p. 68. Based on the definition of Ise Jingū's auxiliary shrines, it was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, i.e., before {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|qq=}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. In ancient times, Tanoya Shrine was subject to the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|lt=Shikinen Sengū|ja|式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}}, rebuilt every 20 years, just like the Inner Shrine. However, after an order to rebuild the shrine in {{ill|Ryakuō|ja|暦応|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryakuō|qq=}} 3 (1340), no further records of reconstruction exist, and both the rituals and the shrine site fell into obscurity. The shrine was restored on {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbun|qq=}} 3, 8th day of the 9th month (October 8, 1663, Gregorian calendar) by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|qq=}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga). == Rituals == Shinto rituals are held in front of the shrine during major festivals such as the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinensai|qq=}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niinamesai|qq=}} (November). == Access == There are two entrance roads to the shrine{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=Course 19 Tamaki Route 2 - Visiting the Gods of the Ancient Countryside|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2025-45/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Central|qq=}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangu Line|qq=}} ** Approximately 40 minutes on foot from {{ill|Sotoida Station|ja|外城田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotoida Station|qq=}} (~3.2 km). ** Approximately 42 minutes on foot from {{ill|Tamura Station|ja|田丸駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamura Station|qq=}} (~3.4 km). * About 10 minutes from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki Interchange|qq=}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Expressway|qq=}}Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "Tanoya Shrine Tourist Information - Mie Sightseeing" (Accessed November 3, 2013). == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_02.jpg 田乃家神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0705.html 田乃家神社（たのえじんじゃ） 御同座 田乃家御前神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|田乃家神社|version=98200648|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] }} [[ja:田乃家神社]] [[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]] [[sn:anoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]] [[en:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]]

{{神社 |名称= 田乃家神社・田乃家御前神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Tanoe-jinja-Tanoemimae-jinja.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]矢野字宮前387 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 36.4 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 36 | 経度秒 = 16.2 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 田乃家神社：大神御滄川神
田乃家御前神社：御前神 |社格= 田乃家神社：式内社（小）、{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}}摂社
田乃家御前神社：皇大神宮摂社 |創建= |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 当藪 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Tanoe Shrine''' (田乃家神社) is a {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrine)|ja|摂末社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sessha (auxiliary shrine)|qq=}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (Naikū) within {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}}. This article also covers the '''Tanoe-Mimae Shrine''' (田乃家御前神社), which shares the same precinct as Tanoe Shrine. Both shrines enshrine deities that serve as {{ill|guardian deities|ja|守護神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deities|qq=}} of {{ill|farmhouses|ja|農家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/farmhouses|qq=}}Gakken Publishing (2013): page 69. == Overview == This section describes common information about both shrines. Located at {{ill|Tamaki|ja|玉城町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki|qq=}}, {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai District|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}, Yano Aza Miyamae 387Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 211. There is a theory that the Yano area, where the shrine is situated, is the final resting place of {{ill|Princess Wakatara|ja|稚足姫皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Wakatara|qq=}}, who served as a {{ill|Saio|ja|斎王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saio|qq=}}{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.town.meiwa.mie.jp/romantic/saio/saio4.html|title=Sites Associated with the Saio (Outside of Town) - Meiwa Town Office|publisher=Meiwa Town, Mie Prefecture|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2045-21/www.town.meiwa.mie.jp/romantic/saio/saio4.html|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. According to the "Chūnansei Development Area Archaeological Map," there are four {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} (ancient Kofuns) within the precinctsKaneko (1983): p. 61, but field research by Masafumi Kimoto confirmed five. Many {{ill|archaeological sites|ja|遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/archaeological sites|qq=}} from the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yayoi period|qq=}} and {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} have been discovered in the Yano area. Additionally, within the precincts are the "Prohibited Killing Stone" established by the {{ill|Kishu Domain|ja|紀州藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishu Domain|qq=}} in {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōhō|qq=}} 9 (1724) and a water basin dated {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpō|qq=}} 13 (1842){{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area04/03.html?iframe=true&width=1000&height=700|title=Tanoya Gozen Shrine - 125 Shrines｜Sengukan|publisher=Sengukan|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2159-39/www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area04/03.html?iframe=true&width=1000&height=700|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|qq=}} constructed in the {{ill|shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shinmei-zukuri|qq=}} architectural style with a wooden shingle roof, facing south and enclosed by a single layer of {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tamagaki|qq=}} (sacred fence). There is one {{ill|shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shinmei torii|qq=}}. No {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offertory box|qq=}} is present. The shrine grounds cover an area of 13,114 m². == Tanoya Shrine == Tanoya Shrine Ranks 5th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Inner Shrine (Kōtai Jingū)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine (Kōtai Jingū)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}}Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11. It holds a high status among the auxiliary shrines of the Inner ShrineIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 65. Its {{ill|sacred object|ja|神体|1=Shintai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred object|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintai|qq=}} is a {{ill|mirror|ja|鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mirror|qq=}}Kaneko (1983): p. 59. The {{ill|enshrined deity|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|qq=}} is Ōkami Misamukawa-no-Kami (大神御滄川神). The deity is revered as the guardian of {{ill|farmers|ja|農耕|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/farmers|qq=}} and {{ill|households|ja|住宅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/households|qq=}}. The prefix "Ōkami" signifies the extension of the divine virtues of {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}, the main deity of the Inner Shrine, to this region. The shrine name "Tanoya" has several theories regarding its origin: one suggests it means "on the rice field" (田の上), another links it to the place name "Tanabe" (田邊郷), and a third relates it to "Tato" (田戸), referring to rice fields and residences near government-owned paddiesShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 208. == Tanoya Gozen Shrine == Tanoya Gozen Shrine Ranks 6th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine. Its main hall no longer exists, and the deity is now enshrined together with Tanoya ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10. The enshrined deity is Gozen-no-Kami (御前神). Like Ōkami Misamukawa-no-Kami, this deity is also a guardian of farmers. == History == According to the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū Ritual Records|ja|皇大神宮儀式帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū Ritual Records|qq=}}, the shrine was founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Yūryaku|qq=}}Kaneko (1983): p. 68. Based on the definition of Ise Jingū's auxiliary shrines, it was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, i.e., before {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|qq=}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. In ancient times, Tanoya Shrine was subject to the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|lt=Shikinen Sengū|ja|式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}}, rebuilt every 20 years, just like the Inner Shrine. However, after an order to rebuild the shrine in {{ill|Ryakuō|ja|暦応|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryakuō|qq=}} 3 (1340), no further records of reconstruction exist, and both the rituals and the shrine site fell into obscurity. The shrine was restored on {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbun|qq=}} 3, 8th day of the 9th month (October 8, 1663, Gregorian calendar) by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|qq=}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga). == Rituals == Shinto rituals are held in front of the shrine during major festivals such as the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinensai|qq=}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}} (October), and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|lt=Niiname-no-Matsuri|lt=Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niinamesai|qq=}} (November). == Access == There are two entrance roads to the shrine{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=Course 19 Tamaki Route 2 - Visiting the Gods of the Ancient Countryside|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2025-45/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Central|qq=}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangu Line|qq=}} ** Approximately 40 minutes on foot from {{ill|Sotoida Station|ja|外城田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotoida Station|qq=}} (~3.2 km). ** Approximately 42 minutes on foot from {{ill|Tamura Station|ja|田丸駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamura Station|qq=}} (~3.4 km). * About 10 minutes from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki Interchange|qq=}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Expressway|qq=}}Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "Tanoya Shrine Tourist Information - Mie Sightseeing" (Accessed November 3, 2013). == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_02.jpg 田乃家神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0705.html 田乃家神社（たのえじんじゃ） 御同座 田乃家御前神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|田乃家神社|version=98200648|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] }} [[ja:田乃家神社]] [[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]] [[sn:anoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]] [[en:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 田乃家神社・田乃家御前神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Tanoe-jinja-Tanoemimae-jinja.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]矢野字宮前387 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 36.4 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 36 | 経度秒 = 16.2 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 田乃家神社：大神御滄川神
田乃家御前神社：御前神 |社格= 田乃家神社：式内社（小）、{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}}摂社
田乃家御前神社：皇大神宮摂社 |創建= |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 当藪 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Tanoe Shrine''' (田乃家神社) is a {{ill|sessha (auxiliary shrine)|ja|摂末社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sessha (auxiliary shrine)|qq=}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (Naikū) within {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}}. This article also covers the '''Tanoe-Mimae Shrine''' (田乃家御前神社), which shares the same precinct as Tanoe Shrine. Both shrines enshrine deities that serve as {{ill|guardian deities|ja|守護神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deities|qq=}} of {{ill|farmhouses|ja|農家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/farmhouses|qq=}}Gakken Publishing (2013): page 69. == Overview == This section describes common information about both shrines. Located at {{ill|Tamaki|ja|玉城町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki|qq=}}, {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai District|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}, Yano Aza Miyamae 387Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 211. There is a theory that the Yano area, where the shrine is situated, is the final resting place of {{ill|Princess Wakatara|ja|稚足姫皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Wakatara|qq=}}, who served as a {{ill|Saio|ja|斎王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saio|qq=}}{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.town.meiwa.mie.jp/romantic/saio/saio4.html|title=Sites Associated with the Saio (Outside of Town) - Meiwa Town Office|publisher=Meiwa Town, Mie Prefecture|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2045-21/www.town.meiwa.mie.jp/romantic/saio/saio4.html|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. According to the "Chūnansei Development Area Archaeological Map," there are four {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} (ancient Kofuns) within the precinctsKaneko (1983): p. 61, but field research by Masafumi Kimoto confirmed five. Many {{ill|archaeological sites|ja|遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/archaeological sites|qq=}} from the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yayoi period|qq=}} and {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} have been discovered in the Yano area. Additionally, within the precincts are the "Prohibited Killing Stone" established by the {{ill|Kishu Domain|ja|紀州藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishu Domain|qq=}} in {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōhō|qq=}} 9 (1724) and a water basin dated {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpō|qq=}} 13 (1842){{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area04/03.html?iframe=true&width=1000&height=700|title=Tanoya Gozen Shrine - 125 Shrines｜Sengukan|publisher=Sengukan|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2159-39/www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area04/03.html?iframe=true&width=1000&height=700|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|qq=}} constructed in the {{ill|shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shinmei-zukuri|qq=}} architectural style with a wooden shingle roof, facing south and enclosed by a single layer of {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tamagaki|qq=}} (sacred fence). There is one {{ill|shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shinmei torii|qq=}}. No {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offertory box|qq=}} is present. The shrine grounds cover an area of 13,114 m². == Tanoya Shrine == Tanoya Shrine Ranks 5th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Inner Shrine (Kōtai Jingū)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine (Kōtai Jingū)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}}Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11. It holds a high status among the auxiliary shrines of the Inner ShrineIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 65. Its {{ill|sacred object|ja|神体|1=Shintai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred object|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintai|qq=}} is a {{ill|mirror|ja|鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mirror|qq=}}Kaneko (1983): p. 59. The {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deity|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|qq=}} is Ōkami Misamukawa-no-Kami (大神御滄川神). The deity is revered as the guardian of {{ill|farmers|ja|農耕|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/farmers|qq=}} and {{ill|households|ja|住宅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/households|qq=}}. The prefix "Ōkami" signifies the extension of the divine virtues of {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}, the main deity of the Inner Shrine, to this region. The shrine name "Tanoya" has several theories regarding its origin: one suggests it means "on the rice field" (田の上), another links it to the place name "Tanabe" (田邊郷), and a third relates it to "Tato" (田戸), referring to rice fields and residences near government-owned paddiesShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 208. == Tanoya Gozen Shrine == Tanoya Gozen Shrine Ranks 6th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine. Its main hall no longer exists, and the deity is now enshrined together with Tanoya ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10. The enshrined deity is Gozen-no-Kami (御前神). Like Ōkami Misamukawa-no-Kami, this deity is also a guardian of farmers. == History == According to the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū Ritual Records|ja|皇大神宮儀式帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū Ritual Records|qq=}}, the shrine was founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Yūryaku|qq=}}Kaneko (1983): p. 68. Based on the definition of Ise Jingū's auxiliary shrines, it was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, i.e., before {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|qq=}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. In ancient times, Tanoya Shrine was subject to the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|lt=Shikinen Sengū|ja|式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}}, rebuilt every 20 years, just like the Inner Shrine. However, after an order to rebuild the shrine in {{ill|Ryakuō|ja|暦応|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryakuō|qq=}} 3 (1340), no further records of reconstruction exist, and both the rituals and the shrine site fell into obscurity. The shrine was restored on {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbun|qq=}} 3, 8th day of the 9th month (October 8, 1663, Gregorian calendar) by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|qq=}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga). == Rituals == Shinto rituals are held in front of the shrine during major festivals such as the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinensai|qq=}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}} (October), and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|lt=Niiname-no-Matsuri|lt=Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niinamesai|qq=}} (November). == Access == There are two entrance roads to the shrine{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=Course 19 Tamaki Route 2 - Visiting the Gods of the Ancient Countryside|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2025-45/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. * {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Central|qq=}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangu Line|qq=}} ** Approximately 40 minutes on foot from {{ill|Sotoida Station|ja|外城田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotoida Station|qq=}} (~3.2 km). ** Approximately 42 minutes on foot from {{ill|Tamura Station|ja|田丸駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamura Station|qq=}} (~3.4 km). * About 10 minutes from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki Interchange|qq=}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Expressway|qq=}}Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "Tanoya Shrine Tourist Information - Mie Sightseeing" (Accessed November 3, 2013). == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_02.jpg 田乃家神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0705.html 田乃家神社（たのえじんじゃ） 御同座 田乃家御前神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|田乃家神社|version=98200648|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] }} [[ja:田乃家神社]] [[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]] [[sn:anoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]] [[en:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]]

revision.comment: Bot: replace {{ill}} with enwiki link via Wikidata
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 田乃家神社・田乃家御前神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Tanoe-jinja-Tanoemimae-jinja.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]矢野字宮前387 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 36.4 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 36 | 経度秒 = 16.2 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 田乃家神社：大神御滄川神
田乃家御前神社：御前神 |社格= 田乃家神社：式内社（小）、{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}}摂社
田乃家御前神社：皇大神宮摂社 |創建= |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 当藪 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Tanoe Shrine''' (田乃家神社) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sessha (auxiliary shrine)]] of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (Naikū) within [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. This article also covers the '''Tanoe-Mimae Shrine''' (田乃家御前神社), which shares the same precinct as Tanoe Shrine. Both shrines enshrine deities that serve as {{ill|guardian deities|ja|守護神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deities|qq=}} of [[:en:Farmer|farmhouses]]Gakken Publishing (2013): page 69. == Overview == This section describes common information about both shrines. Located at [[:en:Tamaki, Mie|Tamaki]], [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Yano Aza Miyamae 387Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 211. There is a theory that the Yano area, where the shrine is situated, is the final resting place of {{ill|Princess Wakatara|ja|稚足姫皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Wakatara|qq=}}, who served as a [[:en:Saiō|Saio]]{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.town.meiwa.mie.jp/romantic/saio/saio4.html|title=Sites Associated with the Saio (Outside of Town) - Meiwa Town Office|publisher=Meiwa Town, Mie Prefecture|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2045-21/www.town.meiwa.mie.jp/romantic/saio/saio4.html|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. According to the "Chūnansei Development Area Archaeological Map," there are four [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) within the precinctsKaneko (1983): p. 61, but field research by Masafumi Kimoto confirmed five. Many [[:en:Archaeological site|archaeological sites]] from the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] and [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] have been discovered in the Yano area. Additionally, within the precincts are the "Prohibited Killing Stone" established by the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishu Domain]] in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 9 (1724) and a water basin dated [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 13 (1842){{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area04/03.html?iframe=true&width=1000&height=700|title=Tanoya Gozen Shrine - 125 Shrines｜Sengukan|publisher=Sengukan|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2159-39/www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area04/03.html?iframe=true&width=1000&height=700|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|qq=}} constructed in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|shinmei-zukuri]] architectural style with a wooden shingle roof, facing south and enclosed by a single layer of [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] (sacred fence). There is one {{ill|shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shinmei torii|qq=}}. No {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offertory box|qq=}} is present. The shrine grounds cover an area of 13,114 m². == Tanoya Shrine == Tanoya Shrine Ranks 5th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Inner Shrine (Kōtai Jingū)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine (Kōtai Jingū)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}}Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11. It holds a high status among the auxiliary shrines of the Inner ShrineIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 65. Its [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] is a [[:en:Mirror|mirror]]Kaneko (1983): p. 59. The {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deity|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|qq=}} is Ōkami Misamukawa-no-Kami (大神御滄川神). The deity is revered as the guardian of {{ill|farmers|ja|農耕|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/farmers|qq=}} and [[:en:Dwelling|households]]. The prefix "Ōkami" signifies the extension of the divine virtues of {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}, the main deity of the Inner Shrine, to this region. The shrine name "Tanoya" has several theories regarding its origin: one suggests it means "on the rice field" (田の上), another links it to the place name "Tanabe" (田邊郷), and a third relates it to "Tato" (田戸), referring to rice fields and residences near government-owned paddiesShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 208. == Tanoya Gozen Shrine == Tanoya Gozen Shrine Ranks 6th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine. Its main hall no longer exists, and the deity is now enshrined together with Tanoya ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10. The enshrined deity is Gozen-no-Kami (御前神). Like Ōkami Misamukawa-no-Kami, this deity is also a guardian of farmers. == History == According to the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū Ritual Records|ja|皇大神宮儀式帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū Ritual Records|qq=}}, the shrine was founded during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]Kaneko (1983): p. 68. Based on the definition of Ise Jingū's auxiliary shrines, it was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, i.e., before {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|qq=}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. In ancient times, Tanoya Shrine was subject to the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|lt=Shikinen Sengū|ja|式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}}, rebuilt every 20 years, just like the Inner Shrine. However, after an order to rebuild the shrine in [[:en:Ryakuō|Ryakuō]] 3 (1340), no further records of reconstruction exist, and both the rituals and the shrine site fell into obscurity. The shrine was restored on [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 3, 8th day of the 9th month (October 8, 1663, Gregorian calendar) by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|qq=}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga). == Rituals == Shinto rituals are held in front of the shrine during major festivals such as the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June, December), [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October), and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] (November). == Access == There are two entrance roads to the shrine{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=Course 19 Tamaki Route 2 - Visiting the Gods of the Ancient Countryside|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2025-45/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. * [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]] ** Approximately 40 minutes on foot from [[:en:Tokida Station|Sotoida Station]] (~3.2 km). ** Approximately 42 minutes on foot from [[:en:Tamaru Station|Tamura Station]] (~3.4 km). * About 10 minutes from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]]Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "Tanoya Shrine Tourist Information - Mie Sightseeing" (Accessed November 3, 2013). == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_02.jpg 田乃家神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0705.html 田乃家神社（たのえじんじゃ） 御同座 田乃家御前神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|田乃家神社|version=98200648|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] }} [[ja:田乃家神社]] [[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]] [[sn:anoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]] [[en:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]]
