{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q17212120]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:51, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称= 田乃家神社・田乃家御前神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Tanoe-jinja-Tanoemimae-jinja.jpg |所在地= [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]矢野字宮前387 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 36.4 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 36 | 経度秒 = 16.2 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 田乃家神社：大神御滄川神
田乃家御前神社：御前神 |社格= 田乃家神社：式内社（小）、{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}摂社
田乃家御前神社：皇大神宮摂社 |創建= |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 当藪 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] |prefecture= {{ill|Tamaki|qid=Q587348}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.476778|N|136.6045|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }} '''Tanoe Shrine''' (田乃家神社) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sessha (auxiliary shrine)]] of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Naikū) within [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. This article also covers the '''Tanoe-Mimae Shrine''' (田乃家御前神社), which shares the same precinct as Tanoe Shrine. Both shrines enshrine deities that serve as {{ill|guardian deities|ja|守護神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deities|qq=}} of [[:en:Farmer|farmhouses]]Gakken Publishing (2013): page 69. == Overview == This section describes common information about both shrines. Located at [[:en:Tamaki, Mie|Tamaki]], [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Yano Aza Miyamae 387Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 211. There is a theory that the Yano area, where the shrine is situated, is the final resting place of {{ill|Princess Wakatara|ja|稚足姫皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Wakatara|qq=|1=Wakatarashihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55533316}}, who served as a [[:en:Saiō|Saio]]{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.town.meiwa.mie.jp/romantic/saio/saio4.html|title=Sites Associated with the Saio (Outside of Town) - Meiwa Town Office|publisher=Meiwa Town, Mie Prefecture|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2045-21/www.town.meiwa.mie.jp/romantic/saio/saio4.html|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. According to the "Chūnansei Development Area Archaeological Map," there are four [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) within the precinctsKaneko (1983): p. 61, but field research by Masafumi Kimoto confirmed five. Many [[:en:Archaeological site|archaeological sites]] from the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] and [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] have been discovered in the Yano area. Additionally, within the precincts are the "Prohibited Killing Stone" established by the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishu Domain]] in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 9 (1724) and a water basin dated [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 13 (1842){{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area04/03.html?iframe=true&width=1000&height=700|title=Tanoya Gozen Shrine - 125 Shrines｜Sengukan|publisher=Sengukan|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2159-39/www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area04/03.html?iframe=true&width=1000&height=700|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} constructed in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|shinmei-zukuri]] architectural style with a wooden shingle roof, facing south and enclosed by a single layer of [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] (sacred fence). There is one {{ill|shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shinmei torii|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥居|1=torii|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q234731}}. No {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offertory box|qq=|qid=Q135491444}} is present. The shrine grounds cover an area of 13,114 m². == Tanoya Shrine == Tanoya Shrine Ranks 5th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Inner Shrine (Kōtai Jingū)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine (Kōtai Jingū)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11. It holds a high status among the auxiliary shrines of the Inner ShrineIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 65. Its [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] is a [[:en:Mirror|mirror]]Kaneko (1983): p. 59. The {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deity|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|qq=}} is Ōkami Misamukawa-no-Kami (大神御滄川神). The deity is revered as the guardian of {{ill|farmers|ja|農耕|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/farmers|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=farming|WD=Q24886094}} and [[:en:Dwelling|households]]. The prefix "Ōkami" signifies the extension of the divine virtues of {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}, the main deity of the Inner Shrine, to this region. The shrine name "Tanoya" has several theories regarding its origin: one suggests it means "on the rice field" (田の上), another links it to the place name "Tanabe" (田邊郷), and a third relates it to "Tato" (田戸), referring to rice fields and residences near government-owned paddiesShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 208. == Tanoya Gozen Shrine == Tanoya Gozen Shrine Ranks 6th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine. Its main hall no longer exists, and the deity is now enshrined together with Tanoya ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10. The enshrined deity is Gozen-no-Kami (御前神). Like Ōkami Misamukawa-no-Kami, this deity is also a guardian of farmers. == History == According to the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū Ritual Records|ja|皇大神宮儀式帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū Ritual Records|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延暦儀式帳|1=Enryaku Gishikichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109596938}}, the shrine was founded during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]Kaneko (1983): p. 68. Based on the definition of Ise Jingū's auxiliary shrines, it was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, i.e., before {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|qq=|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. In ancient times, Tanoya Shrine was subject to the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|lt=Shikinen Sengū|ja|式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 遷宮}}, rebuilt every 20 years, just like the Inner Shrine. However, after an order to rebuild the shrine in [[:en:Ryakuō|Ryakuō]] 3 (1340), no further records of reconstruction exist, and both the rituals and the shrine site fell into obscurity. The shrine was restored on [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 3, 8th day of the 9th month (October 8, 1663, Gregorian calendar) by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|qq=|1=Kiyonaga Kawabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92924151}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga). == Rituals == Shinto rituals are held in front of the shrine during major festivals such as the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June, December), [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October), and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] (November). == Access == There are two entrance roads to the shrine{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=Course 19 Tamaki Route 2 - Visiting the Gods of the Ancient Countryside|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2025-45/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. * [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]] ** Approximately 40 minutes on foot from [[:en:Tokida Station|Sotoida Station]] (~3.2 km). ** Approximately 42 minutes on foot from [[:en:Tamaru Station|Tamura Station]] (~3.4 km). * About 10 minutes from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki Interchange|qq=|1=Tamaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572465}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]]Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "Tanoya Shrine Tourist Information - Mie Sightseeing" (Accessed November 3, 2013). == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_02.jpg 田乃家神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0705.html 田乃家神社（たのえじんじゃ） 御同座 田乃家御前神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|田乃家神社|version=98200648|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]][[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] }} [[ja:田乃家神社]] [[sn:Tanoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]] [[sn:anoe and Tanoe-Mimae Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Tamaki Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tamaki]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q17212120}}

No {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offertory box|qq=|qid=Q135491444}} is present. In ancient times, Tanoya Shrine was subject to the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|lt=Shikinen Sengū|ja|式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 遷宮}}, rebuilt every 20 years, just like the Inner Shrine. However, after an order to rebuild the shrine in [[:en:Ryakuō|Ryakuō]] 3 (1340), no further records of reconstruction exist, and both the rituals and the shrine site fell into obscurity. The shrine was restored on [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 3, 8th day of the 9th month (October 8, 1663, Gregorian calendar) by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|qq=|1=Kiyonaga Kawabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92924151}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga). == Rituals == Shinto rituals are held in front of the shrine during major festivals such as the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June, December), [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October), and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] (November). == Access == There are two entrance roads to the shrine{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=Course 19 Tamaki Route 2 - Visiting the Gods of the Ancient Countryside|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2025-45/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. * [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]] ** Approximately 40 minutes on foot from [[:en:Tokida Station|Sotoida Station]] (~3.2 km). ** Approximately 42 minutes on foot from [[:en:Tamaru Station|Tamura Station]] (~3.4 km). * About 10 minutes from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki Interchange|qq=|1=Tamaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572465}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]]Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "Tanoya Shrine Tourist Information - Mie Sightseeing" (Accessed November 3, 2013). == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_02.jpg 田乃家神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0705.html 田乃家神社（たのえじんじゃ） 御同座 田乃家御前神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|田乃家神社|version=98200648|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] }} [[ja:田乃家神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tamaki Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tamaki]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q17212120}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q17212120]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:51, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称= 田乃家神社・田乃家御前神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Tanoe-jinja-Tanoemimae-jinja.jpg |所在地= [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]矢野字宮前387 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 36.4 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 36 | 経度秒 = 16.2 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 田乃家神社：大神御滄川神
田乃家御前神社：御前神 |社格= 田乃家神社：式内社（小）、{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}摂社
田乃家御前神社：皇大神宮摂社 |創建= |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 当藪 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] |prefecture= {{ill|Tamaki|qid=Q587348}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.476778|N|136.6045|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }} '''Tanoe Shrine''' (田乃家神社) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sessha (auxiliary shrine)]] of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Naikū) within [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. This article also covers the '''Tanoe-Mimae Shrine''' (田乃家御前神社), which shares the same precinct as Tanoe Shrine. Both shrines enshrine deities that serve as {{ill|guardian deities|ja|守護神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deities|qq=}} of [[:en:Farmer|farmhouses]]Gakken Publishing (2013): page 69. == Overview == This section describes common information about both shrines. Located at [[:en:Tamaki, Mie|Tamaki]], [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Yano Aza Miyamae 387Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 211. There is a theory that the Yano area, where the shrine is situated, is the final resting place of {{ill|Princess Wakatara|ja|稚足姫皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Wakatara|qq=|1=Wakatarashihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55533316}}, who served as a [[:en:Saiō|Saio]]{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.town.meiwa.mie.jp/romantic/saio/saio4.html|title=Sites Associated with the Saio (Outside of Town) - Meiwa Town Office|publisher=Meiwa Town, Mie Prefecture|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2045-21/www.town.meiwa.mie.jp/romantic/saio/saio4.html|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. According to the "Chūnansei Development Area Archaeological Map," there are four [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) within the precinctsKaneko (1983): p. 61, but field research by Masafumi Kimoto confirmed five. Many [[:en:Archaeological site|archaeological sites]] from the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] and [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] have been discovered in the Yano area. Additionally, within the precincts are the "Prohibited Killing Stone" established by the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishu Domain]] in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 9 (1724) and a water basin dated [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 13 (1842){{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area04/03.html?iframe=true&width=1000&height=700|title=Tanoya Gozen Shrine - 125 Shrines｜Sengukan|publisher=Sengukan|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2159-39/www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area04/03.html?iframe=true&width=1000&height=700|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} constructed in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|shinmei-zukuri]] architectural style with a wooden shingle roof, facing south and enclosed by a single layer of [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] (sacred fence). There is one {{ill|shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shinmei torii|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥居|1=torii|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q234731}}. No {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offertory box|qq=|qid=Q135491444}} is present. The shrine grounds cover an area of 13,114 m². == Tanoya Shrine == Tanoya Shrine Ranks 5th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Inner Shrine (Kōtai Jingū)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine (Kōtai Jingū)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11. It holds a high status among the auxiliary shrines of the Inner ShrineIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 65. Its [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] is a [[:en:Mirror|mirror]]Kaneko (1983): p. 59. The {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deity|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|qq=}} is Ōkami Misamukawa-no-Kami (大神御滄川神). The deity is revered as the guardian of {{ill|farmers|ja|農耕|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/farmers|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=farming|WD=Q24886094}} and [[:en:Dwelling|households]]. The prefix "Ōkami" signifies the extension of the divine virtues of {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}, the main deity of the Inner Shrine, to this region. The shrine name "Tanoya" has several theories regarding its origin: one suggests it means "on the rice field" (田の上), another links it to the place name "Tanabe" (田邊郷), and a third relates it to "Tato" (田戸), referring to rice fields and residences near government-owned paddiesShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 208. == Tanoya Gozen Shrine == Tanoya Gozen Shrine Ranks 6th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine. Its main hall no longer exists, and the deity is now enshrined together with Tanoya ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10. The enshrined deity is Gozen-no-Kami (御前神). Like Ōkami Misamukawa-no-Kami, this deity is also a guardian of farmers. == History == According to the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū Ritual Records|ja|皇大神宮儀式帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū Ritual Records|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延暦儀式帳|1=Enryaku Gishikichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109596938}}, the shrine was founded during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]Kaneko (1983): p. 68. Based on the definition of Ise Jingū's auxiliary shrines, it was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, i.e., before {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|qq=|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. In ancient times, Tanoya Shrine was subject to the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|lt=Shikinen Sengū|ja|式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 遷宮}}, rebuilt every 20 years, just like the Inner Shrine. However, after an order to rebuild the shrine in [[:en:Ryakuō|Ryakuō]] 3 (1340), no further records of reconstruction exist, and both the rituals and the shrine site fell into obscurity. The shrine was restored on [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 3, 8th day of the 9th month (October 8, 1663, Gregorian calendar) by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|qq=|1=Kiyonaga Kawabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92924151}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga). == Rituals == Shinto rituals are held in front of the shrine during major festivals such as the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June, December), [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October), and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] (November). == Access == There are two entrance roads to the shrine{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=Course 19 Tamaki Route 2 - Visiting the Gods of the Ancient Countryside|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2025-45/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. * [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]] ** Approximately 40 minutes on foot from [[:en:Tokida Station|Sotoida Station]] (~3.2 km). ** Approximately 42 minutes on foot from [[:en:Tamaru Station|Tamura Station]] (~3.4 km). * About 10 minutes from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki Interchange|qq=|1=Tamaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572465}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]]Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "Tanoya Shrine Tourist Information - Mie Sightseeing" (Accessed November 3, 2013). == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_02.jpg 田乃家神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0705.html 田乃家神社（たのえじんじゃ） 御同座 田乃家御前神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|田乃家神社|version=98200648|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] }} [[ja:田乃家神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tamaki Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tamaki]] [[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q17212120}}

There is one {{ill|shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shinmei torii|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥居|1=torii|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q234731}}. No {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offertory box|qq=|qid=Q135491444}} is present. The shrine grounds cover an area of 13,114 m². == Tanoya Shrine == Tanoya Shrine Ranks 5th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Inner Shrine (Kōtai Jingū)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine (Kōtai Jingū)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11. It holds a high status among the auxiliary shrines of the Inner ShrineIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 65. Its [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] is a [[:en:Mirror|mirror]]Kaneko (1983): p. 59. The {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deity|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|qq=}} is Ōkami Misamukawa-no-Kami (大神御滄川神). The deity is revered as the guardian of {{ill|farmers|ja|農耕|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/farmers|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=farming|WD=Q24886094}} and [[:en:Dwelling|households]]. The prefix "Ōkami" signifies the extension of the divine virtues of {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}, the main deity of the Inner Shrine, to this region. The shrine name "Tanoya" has several theories regarding its origin: one suggests it means "on the rice field" (田の上), another links it to the place name "Tanabe" (田邊郷), and a third relates it to "Tato" (田戸), referring to rice fields and residences near government-owned paddiesShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 208. == Tanoya Gozen Shrine == Tanoya Gozen Shrine Ranks 6th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine. Its main hall no longer exists, and the deity is now enshrined together with Tanoya ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10. The enshrined deity is Gozen-no-Kami (御前神). Like Ōkami Misamukawa-no-Kami, this deity is also a guardian of farmers. == History == According to the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū Ritual Records|ja|皇大神宮儀式帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū Ritual Records|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延暦儀式帳|1=Enryaku Gishikichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109596938}}, the shrine was founded during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]Kaneko (1983): p. 68. Based on the definition of Ise Jingū's auxiliary shrines, it was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, i.e., before {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|qq=|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. In ancient times, Tanoya Shrine was subject to the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|lt=Shikinen Sengū|ja|式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 遷宮}}, rebuilt every 20 years, just like the Inner Shrine. However, after an order to rebuild the shrine in [[:en:Ryakuō|Ryakuō]] 3 (1340), no further records of reconstruction exist, and both the rituals and the shrine site fell into obscurity. The shrine was restored on [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 3, 8th day of the 9th month (October 8, 1663, Gregorian calendar) by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|qq=|1=Kiyonaga Kawabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92924151}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga). == Rituals == Shinto rituals are held in front of the shrine during major festivals such as the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June, December), [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October), and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] (November). == Access == There are two entrance roads to the shrine{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=Course 19 Tamaki Route 2 - Visiting the Gods of the Ancient Countryside|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2025-45/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. * [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]] ** Approximately 40 minutes on foot from [[:en:Tokida Station|Sotoida Station]] (~3.2 km). ** Approximately 42 minutes on foot from [[:en:Tamaru Station|Tamura Station]] (~3.4 km). * About 10 minutes from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki Interchange|qq=|1=Tamaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572465}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]]Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "Tanoya Shrine Tourist Information - Mie Sightseeing" (Accessed November 3, 2013). == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_02.jpg 田乃家神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0705.html 田乃家神社（たのえじんじゃ） 御同座 田乃家御前神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|田乃家神社|version=98200648|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] }} [[ja:田乃家神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tamaki Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tamaki]] {{wikidata link|Q17212120}}

revision.text:
[[ja:田乃家神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q17212120]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:51, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称= 田乃家神社・田乃家御前神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Tanoe-jinja-Tanoemimae-jinja.jpg |所在地= [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]矢野字宮前387 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 36.4 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 36 | 経度秒 = 16.2 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 田乃家神社：大神御滄川神
田乃家御前神社：御前神 |社格= 田乃家神社：式内社（小）、{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}摂社
田乃家御前神社：皇大神宮摂社 |創建= |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 当藪 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] |prefecture= {{ill|Tamaki|qid=Q587348}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.476778|N|136.6045|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }} '''Tanoe Shrine''' (田乃家神社) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sessha (auxiliary shrine)]] of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Naikū) within [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. This article also covers the '''Tanoe-Mimae Shrine''' (田乃家御前神社), which shares the same precinct as Tanoe Shrine. Both shrines enshrine deities that serve as {{ill|guardian deities|ja|守護神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deities|qq=}} of [[:en:Farmer|farmhouses]]Gakken Publishing (2013): page 69. == Overview == This section describes common information about both shrines. Located at [[:en:Tamaki, Mie|Tamaki]], [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Yano Aza Miyamae 387Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 211. There is a theory that the Yano area, where the shrine is situated, is the final resting place of {{ill|Princess Wakatara|ja|稚足姫皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Wakatara|qq=|1=Wakatarashihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55533316}}, who served as a [[:en:Saiō|Saio]]{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.town.meiwa.mie.jp/romantic/saio/saio4.html|title=Sites Associated with the Saio (Outside of Town) - Meiwa Town Office|publisher=Meiwa Town, Mie Prefecture|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2045-21/www.town.meiwa.mie.jp/romantic/saio/saio4.html|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. According to the "Chūnansei Development Area Archaeological Map," there are four [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) within the precinctsKaneko (1983): p. 61, but field research by Masafumi Kimoto confirmed five. Many [[:en:Archaeological site|archaeological sites]] from the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] and [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] have been discovered in the Yano area. Additionally, within the precincts are the "Prohibited Killing Stone" established by the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishu Domain]] in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 9 (1724) and a water basin dated [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 13 (1842){{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area04/03.html?iframe=true&width=1000&height=700|title=Tanoya Gozen Shrine - 125 Shrines｜Sengukan|publisher=Sengukan|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2159-39/www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area04/03.html?iframe=true&width=1000&height=700|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} constructed in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|shinmei-zukuri]] architectural style with a wooden shingle roof, facing south and enclosed by a single layer of [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] (sacred fence). There is one {{ill|shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shinmei torii|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥居|1=torii|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q234731}}. No {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offertory box|qq=|qid=Q135491444}} is present. The shrine grounds cover an area of 13,114 m². == Tanoya Shrine == Tanoya Shrine Ranks 5th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Inner Shrine (Kōtai Jingū)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine (Kōtai Jingū)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11. It holds a high status among the auxiliary shrines of the Inner ShrineIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 65. Its [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] is a [[:en:Mirror|mirror]]Kaneko (1983): p. 59. The {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deity|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|qq=}} is Ōkami Misamukawa-no-Kami (大神御滄川神). The deity is revered as the guardian of {{ill|farmers|ja|農耕|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/farmers|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=farming|WD=Q24886094}} and [[:en:Dwelling|households]]. The prefix "Ōkami" signifies the extension of the divine virtues of {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}, the main deity of the Inner Shrine, to this region. The shrine name "Tanoya" has several theories regarding its origin: one suggests it means "on the rice field" (田の上), another links it to the place name "Tanabe" (田邊郷), and a third relates it to "Tato" (田戸), referring to rice fields and residences near government-owned paddiesShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 208. == Tanoya Gozen Shrine == Tanoya Gozen Shrine Ranks 6th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine. Its main hall no longer exists, and the deity is now enshrined together with Tanoya ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10. The enshrined deity is Gozen-no-Kami (御前神). Like Ōkami Misamukawa-no-Kami, this deity is also a guardian of farmers. == History == According to the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū Ritual Records|ja|皇大神宮儀式帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū Ritual Records|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延暦儀式帳|1=Enryaku Gishikichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109596938}}, the shrine was founded during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]Kaneko (1983): p. 68. Based on the definition of Ise Jingū's auxiliary shrines, it was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, i.e., before {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|qq=|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. In ancient times, Tanoya Shrine was subject to the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|lt=Shikinen Sengū|ja|式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 遷宮}}, rebuilt every 20 years, just like the Inner Shrine. However, after an order to rebuild the shrine in [[:en:Ryakuō|Ryakuō]] 3 (1340), no further records of reconstruction exist, and both the rituals and the shrine site fell into obscurity. The shrine was restored on [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 3, 8th day of the 9th month (October 8, 1663, Gregorian calendar) by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|qq=|1=Kiyonaga Kawabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92924151}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga). == Rituals == Shinto rituals are held in front of the shrine during major festivals such as the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June, December), [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October), and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] (November). == Access == There are two entrance roads to the shrine{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=Course 19 Tamaki Route 2 - Visiting the Gods of the Ancient Countryside|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2025-45/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. * [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]] ** Approximately 40 minutes on foot from [[:en:Tokida Station|Sotoida Station]] (~3.2 km). ** Approximately 42 minutes on foot from [[:en:Tamaru Station|Tamura Station]] (~3.4 km). * About 10 minutes from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki Interchange|qq=|1=Tamaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572465}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]]Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "Tanoya Shrine Tourist Information - Mie Sightseeing" (Accessed November 3, 2013). == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_02.jpg 田乃家神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0705.html 田乃家神社（たのえじんじゃ） 御同座 田乃家御前神社] - 田乃家御前神社：皇大神宮摂社 |創建= |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 当藪 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] |prefecture= {{ill|Tamaki|WD=Q587348}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.476778|N|136.6045|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Tanoe Shrine''' (田乃家神社) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sessha (auxiliary shrine)]] of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Naikū) within [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. This article also covers the '''Tanoe-Mimae Shrine''' (田乃家御前神社), which shares the same precinct as Tanoe Shrine. Both shrines enshrine deities that serve as {{ill|guardian deities|ja|守護神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deities|qq=}} of [[:en:Farmer|farmhouses]]Gakken Publishing (2013): page 69. == Overview == This section describes common information about both shrines. Located at [[:en:Tamaki, Mie|Tamaki]], [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Yano Aza Miyamae 387Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 211. There is a theory that the Yano area, where the shrine is situated, is the final resting place of {{ill|Princess Wakatara|ja|稚足姫皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Wakatara|qq=|1=Wakatarashihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55533316}}, who served as a [[:en:Saiō|Saio]]{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.town.meiwa.mie.jp/romantic/saio/saio4.html|title=Sites Associated with the Saio (Outside of Town) - Meiwa Town Office|publisher=Meiwa Town, Mie Prefecture|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2045-21/www.town.meiwa.mie.jp/romantic/saio/saio4.html|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. According to the "Chūnansei Development Area Archaeological Map," there are four [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) within the precinctsKaneko (1983): p. 61, but field research by Masafumi Kimoto confirmed five. Many [[:en:Archaeological site|archaeological sites]] from the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] and [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] have been discovered in the Yano area. Additionally, within the precincts are the "Prohibited Killing Stone" established by the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishu Domain]] in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 9 (1724) and a water basin dated [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 13 (1842){{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area04/03.html?iframe=true&width=1000&height=700|title=Tanoya Gozen Shrine - 125 Shrines｜Sengukan|publisher=Sengukan|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2159-39/www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area04/03.html?iframe=true&width=1000&height=700|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} constructed in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|shinmei-zukuri]] architectural style with a wooden shingle roof, facing south and enclosed by a single layer of [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] (sacred fence). There is one {{ill|shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shinmei torii|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥居|1=torii|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q234731}}. No {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offertory box|qq=|WD=Q135491444}} is present. The shrine grounds cover an area of 13,114 m². == Tanoya Shrine == Tanoya Shrine Ranks 5th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Inner Shrine (Kōtai Jingū)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine (Kōtai Jingū)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11. It holds a high status among the auxiliary shrines of the Inner ShrineIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 65. Its [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] is a [[:en:Mirror|mirror]]Kaneko (1983): p. 59. The {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deity|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|qq=}} is Ōkami Misamukawa-no-Kami (大神御滄川神). The deity is revered as the guardian of {{ill|farmers|ja|農耕|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/farmers|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=farming|WD=Q24886094}} and [[:en:Dwelling|households]]. The prefix "Ōkami" signifies the extension of the divine virtues of {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}, the main deity of the Inner Shrine, to this region. The shrine name "Tanoya" has several theories regarding its origin: one suggests it means "on the rice field" (田の上), another links it to the place name "Tanabe" (田邊郷), and a third relates it to "Tato" (田戸), referring to rice fields and residences near government-owned paddiesShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 208. == Tanoya Gozen Shrine == Tanoya Gozen Shrine Ranks 6th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine. Its main hall no longer exists, and the deity is now enshrined together with Tanoya ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10. The enshrined deity is Gozen-no-Kami (御前神). Like Ōkami Misamukawa-no-Kami, this deity is also a guardian of farmers. == History == According to the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū Ritual Records|ja|皇大神宮儀式帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū Ritual Records|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延暦儀式帳|1=Enryaku Gishikichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109596938}}, the shrine was founded during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]Kaneko (1983): p. 68. Based on the definition of Ise Jingū's auxiliary shrines, it was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, i.e., before {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|qq=|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. In ancient times, Tanoya Shrine was subject to the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|lt=Shikinen Sengū|ja|式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 遷宮}}, rebuilt every 20 years, just like the Inner Shrine. However, after an order to rebuild the shrine in [[:en:Ryakuō|Ryakuō]] 3 (1340), no further records of reconstruction exist, and both the rituals and the shrine site fell into obscurity. The shrine was restored on [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 3, 8th day of the 9th month (October 8, 1663, Gregorian calendar) by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|qq=|1=Kiyonaga Kawabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92924151}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga). == Rituals == Shinto rituals are held in front of the shrine during major festivals such as the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June, December), [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October), and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] (November). == Access == There are two entrance roads to the shrine{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=Course 19 Tamaki Route 2 - Visiting the Gods of the Ancient Countryside|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2025-45/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. * [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]] ** Approximately 40 minutes on foot from [[:en:Tokida Station|Sotoida Station]] (~3.2 km). ** Approximately 42 minutes on foot from [[:en:Tamaru Station|Tamura Station]] (~3.4 km). * About 10 minutes from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki Interchange|qq=|1=Tamaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572465}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]]Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "Tanoya Shrine Tourist Information - Mie Sightseeing" (Accessed November 3, 2013). == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_02.jpg 田乃家神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0705.html 田乃家神社（たのえじんじゃ） 御同座 田乃家御前神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|田乃家神社|version=98200648|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] }} [[ja:田乃家神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tamaki Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tamaki]] {{wikidata link|Q17212120}}

There is one {{ill|shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shinmei torii|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥居|1=torii|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q234731}}. No {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offertory box|qq=|WD=Q135491444}} is present. The shrine grounds cover an area of 13,114 m². == Tanoya Shrine == Tanoya Shrine Ranks 5th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Inner Shrine (Kōtai Jingū)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine (Kōtai Jingū)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11. The prefix "Ōkami" signifies the extension of the divine virtues of {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}, the main deity of the Inner Shrine, to this region. In ancient times, Tanoya Shrine was subject to the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|lt=Shikinen Sengū|ja|式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 遷宮}}, rebuilt every 20 years, just like the Inner Shrine. However, after an order to rebuild the shrine in [[:en:Ryakuō|Ryakuō]] 3 (1340), no further records of reconstruction exist, and both the rituals and the shrine site fell into obscurity. The shrine was restored on [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 3, 8th day of the 9th month (October 8, 1663, Gregorian calendar) by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|qq=|1=Kiyonaga Kawabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92924151}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga). == Rituals == Shinto rituals are held in front of the shrine during major festivals such as the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June, December), [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October), and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] (November). == Access == There are two entrance roads to the shrine{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=Course 19 Tamaki Route 2 - Visiting the Gods of the Ancient Countryside|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2025-45/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. * [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]] ** Approximately 40 minutes on foot from [[:en:Tokida Station|Sotoida Station]] (~3.2 km). ** Approximately 42 minutes on foot from [[:en:Tamaru Station|Tamura Station]] (~3.4 km). * About 10 minutes from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki Interchange|qq=|1=Tamaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572465}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]]Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "Tanoya Shrine Tourist Information - Mie Sightseeing" (Accessed November 3, 2013). == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_02.jpg 田乃家神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0705.html 田乃家神社（たのえじんじゃ） 御同座 田乃家御前神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|田乃家神社|version=98200648|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] }} [[ja:田乃家神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tamaki Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tamaki]] {{wikidata link|Q17212120}}

revision.text:
[[ja:田乃家神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q17212120]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:51, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称= 田乃家神社・田乃家御前神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Tanoe-jinja-Tanoemimae-jinja.jpg |所在地= [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]矢野字宮前387 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 36.4 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 36 | 経度秒 = 16.2 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 田乃家神社：大神御滄川神
田乃家御前神社：御前神 |社格= 田乃家神社：式内社（小）、{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}摂社
田乃家御前神社：皇大神宮摂社 |創建= |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 当藪 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] |prefecture= {{ill|Tamaki|WD=Q587348}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.476778|N|136.6045|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Tanoe Shrine''' (田乃家神社) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sessha (auxiliary shrine)]] of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Naikū) within [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. This article also covers the '''Tanoe-Mimae Shrine''' (田乃家御前神社), which shares the same precinct as Tanoe Shrine. Both shrines enshrine deities that serve as {{ill|guardian deities|ja|守護神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deities|qq=}} of [[:en:Farmer|farmhouses]]Gakken Publishing (2013): page 69. == Overview == This section describes common information about both shrines. Located at [[:en:Tamaki, Mie|Tamaki]], [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Yano Aza Miyamae 387Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 211. There is a theory that the Yano area, where the shrine is situated, is the final resting place of {{ill|Princess Wakatara|ja|稚足姫皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Wakatara|1=Wakatarashihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55533316}}, who served as a [[:en:Saiō|Saio]]{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.town.meiwa.mie.jp/romantic/saio/saio4.html|title=Sites Associated with the Saio (Outside of Town) - Meiwa Town Office|publisher=Meiwa Town, Mie Prefecture|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2045-21/www.town.meiwa.mie.jp/romantic/saio/saio4.html|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. According to the "Chūnansei Development Area Archaeological Map," there are four [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) within the precinctsKaneko (1983): p. 61, but field research by Masafumi Kimoto confirmed five. Many [[:en:Archaeological site|archaeological sites]] from the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] and [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] have been discovered in the Yano area. Additionally, within the precincts are the "Prohibited Killing Stone" established by the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishu Domain]] in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 9 (1724) and a water basin dated [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 13 (1842){{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area04/03.html?iframe=true&width=1000&height=700|title=Tanoya Gozen Shrine - 125 Shrines｜Sengukan|publisher=Sengukan|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2159-39/www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area04/03.html?iframe=true&width=1000&height=700|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} constructed in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|shinmei-zukuri]] architectural style with a wooden shingle roof, facing south and enclosed by a single layer of [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] (sacred fence). There is one {{ill|shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shinmei torii|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥居|1=torii|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q234731}}. No {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offertory box|WD=Q135491444}} is present. The shrine grounds cover an area of 13,114 m². == Tanoya Shrine == Tanoya Shrine Ranks 5th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the {{ill|Inner Shrine (Kōtai Jingū)|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine (Kōtai Jingū)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11. It holds a high status among the auxiliary shrines of the Inner ShrineIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 65. Its [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] is a [[:en:Mirror|mirror]]Kaneko (1983): p. 59. The {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deity|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|qq=}} is Ōkami Misamukawa-no-Kami (大神御滄川神). The deity is revered as the guardian of {{ill|farmers|ja|農耕|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/farmers|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=farming|WD=Q24886094}} and [[:en:Dwelling|households]]. The prefix "Ōkami" signifies the extension of the divine virtues of {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}, the main deity of the Inner Shrine, to this region. The shrine name "Tanoya" has several theories regarding its origin: one suggests it means "on the rice field" (田の上), another links it to the place name "Tanabe" (田邊郷), and a third relates it to "Tato" (田戸), referring to rice fields and residences near government-owned paddiesShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 208. == Tanoya Gozen Shrine == Tanoya Gozen Shrine Ranks 6th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine. Its main hall no longer exists, and the deity is now enshrined together with Tanoya ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10. The enshrined deity is Gozen-no-Kami (御前神). Like Ōkami Misamukawa-no-Kami, this deity is also a guardian of farmers. == History == According to the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū Ritual Records|ja|皇大神宮儀式帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū Ritual Records|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延暦儀式帳|1=Enryaku Gishikichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109596938}}, the shrine was founded during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]Kaneko (1983): p. 68. Based on the definition of Ise Jingū's auxiliary shrines, it was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, i.e., before {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. In ancient times, Tanoya Shrine was subject to the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|lt=Shikinen Sengū|ja|式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 遷宮}}, rebuilt every 20 years, just like the Inner Shrine. However, after an order to rebuild the shrine in [[:en:Ryakuō|Ryakuō]] 3 (1340), no further records of reconstruction exist, and both the rituals and the shrine site fell into obscurity. The shrine was restored on [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 3, 8th day of the 9th month (October 8, 1663, Gregorian calendar) by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|1=Kiyonaga Kawabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92924151}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga). == Rituals == Shinto rituals are held in front of the shrine during major festivals such as the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June, December), [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October), and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] (November). == Access == There are two entrance roads to the shrine{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=Course 19 Tamaki Route 2 - Visiting the Gods of the Ancient Countryside|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2025-45/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. * [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]] ** Approximately 40 minutes on foot from [[:en:Tokida Station|Sotoida Station]] (~3.2 km). ** Approximately 42 minutes on foot from [[:en:Tamaru Station|Tamura Station]] (~3.4 km). * About 10 minutes from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki Interchange|1=Tamaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572465}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]]Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "Tanoya Shrine Tourist Information - Mie Sightseeing" (Accessed November 3, 2013). == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_02.jpg 田乃家神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0705.html 田乃家神社（たのえじんじゃ） 御同座 田乃家御前神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|田乃家神社|version=98200648|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] }} [[ja:田乃家神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tamaki Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tamaki]] {{wikidata link|Q17212120}}

No {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offertory box|WD=Q135491444}} is present. The {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deity|ja|祭神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deity|qq=}} is Ōkami Misamukawa-no-Kami (大神御滄川神). The deity is revered as the guardian of {{ill|farmers|ja|農耕|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/farmers|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=farming|WD=Q24886094}} and [[:en:Dwelling|households]]. The prefix "Ōkami" signifies the extension of the divine virtues of {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}, the main deity of the Inner Shrine, to this region. The shrine name "Tanoya" has several theories regarding its origin: one suggests it means "on the rice field" (田の上), another links it to the place name "Tanabe" (田邊郷), and a third relates it to "Tato" (田戸), referring to rice fields and residences near government-owned paddiesShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 208. == Tanoya Gozen Shrine == Tanoya Gozen Shrine Ranks 6th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine. Its main hall no longer exists, and the deity is now enshrined together with Tanoya ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10. The enshrined deity is Gozen-no-Kami (御前神). Like Ōkami Misamukawa-no-Kami, this deity is also a guardian of farmers. == History == According to the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū Ritual Records|ja|皇大神宮儀式帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū Ritual Records|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延暦儀式帳|1=Enryaku Gishikichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109596938}}, the shrine was founded during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]Kaneko (1983): p. 68. Based on the definition of Ise Jingū's auxiliary shrines, it was established before the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, i.e., before {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} 5 (927)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22. In ancient times, Tanoya Shrine was subject to the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|lt=Shikinen Sengū|ja|式年遷宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 遷宮}}, rebuilt every 20 years, just like the Inner Shrine. However, after an order to rebuild the shrine in [[:en:Ryakuō|Ryakuō]] 3 (1340), no further records of reconstruction exist, and both the rituals and the shrine site fell into obscurity. The shrine was restored on [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 3, 8th day of the 9th month (October 8, 1663, Gregorian calendar) by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|1=Kiyonaga Kawabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92924151}} (Ōnakatomi Masanaga). == Rituals == Shinto rituals are held in front of the shrine during major festivals such as the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June, December), [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October), and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] (November). == Access == There are two entrance roads to the shrine{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|title=Course 19 Tamaki Route 2 - Visiting the Gods of the Ancient Countryside|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|language=Japanese|accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2025-45/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html|archivedate=2013-11-03}}. * [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]] ** Approximately 40 minutes on foot from [[:en:Tokida Station|Sotoida Station]] (~3.2 km). ** Approximately 42 minutes on foot from [[:en:Tamaru Station|Tamura Station]] (~3.4 km). * About 10 minutes from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki Interchange|1=Tamaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572465}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]]Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "Tanoya Shrine Tourist Information - Mie Sightseeing" (Accessed November 3, 2013). == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_02.jpg 田乃家神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0705.html 田乃家神社（たのえじんじゃ） 御同座 田乃家御前神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|田乃家神社|version=98200648|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] }} [[ja:田乃家神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tamaki Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tamaki]] {{wikidata link|Q17212120}}
